Coat of arms of the Kingdom of the Two Segregated Sicilies
Museo di Palazzo Alunno, Palermo
The coat of arms of the Kingdom of the Two Segregated Sicilies (Italian: Corona di Two Sicilies) was officially adopted on January 25, 1816 (the day after the unification of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies) by King Joachim Murat,[1] following the abolition of the former arms in 1734.
The escutcheon is divided in four parts: 1. Gilded or (antique) purple, the color of the House of Anjou, the medieval royal house of the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies; 2. Gules, the field representing the Kingdom of Naples; 3. Or, the field representing the Kingdom of Sicily; 4. A cross argent, recalling the Aragon-Sicilian dynastic inheritance.
Above the escutcheon and divides the coat of arms horizontally, two coronets: in the first one, argent, eight fleurs-de-lys azure, the royal arms of the House of Bourbon; in the second one, gules, seven olive branches proper

The Borneo Highland Orangutan (Pongo lowi) is one of two species of orangutans, the other being the Bornean orangutan (Pongo pygmaeus). They are the most humanlike of all the great apes. There is no evidence that they are endangered in the wild.

These orangutan subspecies are the northernmost great apes. The Borneo Highland Orangutan is found in a small region of northwestern Sabah, the state of Malaysia on the island of Borneo. It is also known as the Northwestern Orangutan. The other orangutan species is more widespread across Southeast Asia.

Subspecies
Pongo lowi lowi
Pongo lowi pygmaeus

Picking wild blueberries is a fun activity that can be done by people of all ages!  It's important to go out into the wildblueberry fields with proper safety equipment such as a helmet, track pants, work gloves, and goggles.  It's best to go out into the field during the middle of the day when there is more sunlight.  Beginning in July, there are rows and rows of blue plastic crates that are stacked on top of each other.  These are the wild blueberry fields.  It's important to walk gingerly throughout the field because there could be uneven terrain.  The bush itself is delicate and has many brambles that shoot out from it.   It's easy to get "trapped" or jammed up while wandering through the field.  You will need to drive your car between two rows of crates in order to get your vehicle out of the field.  Be careful not to go over the edges of the field because there is about 6 inches of space below the first crate with no protection.  It's also important to stay with the group and walk in a straight line to prevent "drift".  Drift is when the bush tips over because of the weight of the fruit

The 2019 UCI Urban Cycling World Championships was the first edition of the UCI Urban Cycling World Championships, held in  Birmingham, UK, from October 16 to 19, 2019. It was hosted by the West Midlands Cycle Forum, with the support of the Birmingham City Council, the West Midlands Combined Authority and the National Health Service.

The event was eligible to be declared a UCI Grade 2 event. Defending champions crowned at the event were:

Men's sprint, road bicycle racing 
Women's sprint, road bicycle racing

The organising committee estimated that the championships would spark a spend of £100 million (approximately €113 million or $133 million) in Birmingham and the West Midlands.

The event attracted many notable cyclists, including retired professionals and Olympic gold medalists. It was also attended by the current men's and women's world road race champions, Annemiek van Vleuten and Anna van der Breggen.

The event was shown live on television in several countries, and attracted a worldwide audience of 300 million.

The 2019 UCI Track Cycling World Championships were the 21st UCI Track Cycling World Championships. to be held in Pruszków, Poland from 27 April 2019 to 1 May 2019. This was the first time that Poland had hosted the event.

This was the first time that mass start events were part of the UCI Track Cycling World Championships. The programme included the men's and women's elite championships, as well as the men's, women's, and junior elite mixed relays.

The previous record number of medals won by a country at a single UCI Track Cycling World Championships was held by the Netherlands, who took home a total of 38 medals in 2016. Great Britain broke their record, by winning 39 medals at these championships.

This tournament was considered to be the last major championships for many athletes, such as Laura Kenny, Elia Viviani, Helen Wyman, Martin, Mads Christensen, Kjeld Nuis, Roy Pieters, and Niklas Larsen.

Go to the OBS website and download the latest version of the software. Once the download is complete, open the file and install the plugin.
After the plugin is installed, open OBS and go to the "Window" menu at the top left of the screen.

Step 3: Allow the Discord plugin to access your microphone and camera.
In the "Discord" panel on the left side of the screen, make sure that the two switches next to your microphone and camera icons are switched to the "On" position.

The Australian city Sydney is located in the country of Australia. It is home to many popular landmarks, such as the Sydney Harbour Bridge and the Sydney Opera House. Visitors from all over the world come to Sydney to enjoy the city's vibrant culture, stunning natural scenery, and delicious cuisine.

Sydney offers a variety of different accommodation options to suit every visitor's needs and preferences. Options include hotels, resorts, apartments, and guesthouses. All of these accommodation options offer their own unique benefits, so it's important to choose the one that best suits your requirements.

One popular accommodation option in Sydney is the serviced apartment. These are fully-furnished and self-contained apartments that are professionally managed. They offer all the amenities of home, including modern kitchen facilities, laundry services, and cable TV. Many serviced apartments also offer additional services, such as airport transfers, housekeeping, and concierge services.

Another popular option is a vacation rental property, such as a house or condominium. These are privately owned homes or condominiums that are rented out to tourists for a fee. Vacation rentals offer the convenience of being located in the city center, close to all the attractions.

Rugby league positions and player positions share similarities, but also differ in several aspects. Both football codes feature squads of up to 13 fielded players, which can be subbed on and off in the case of injuries or during the match as the situation dictates. Within each team, the players are classified as forwards or backs. Typically, a team will field four forwards (three forwards and one of either of: a prop, a hooker, or a loose forward); four backs (three backs and one of either: a winger, a centre, a five-eighth, or a fullback); and one goalkeeper.

Ford has officially unveiled the all-new 2021 Mustang Mach-e all-electric SUV in a historic premiere event led by executives from Ford and Electrify America. The all-electric Mach-e comes to market as the first model from Ford's new all-electric vehicle platform, which will grow to include additional EVs in the coming years.
"All the world's a stage, And all the men and women merely players; They have their act to learn, And they have their exits and entrances," wrote the English poet John Webster in the 1612 work, "The Duchess of Malfi." Webster was writing about the concept of "persona," a Latin term that means "mask" or "appearance." Webster's Duchess of Malfi, for example, observes the correct courtly behavior, but reveals a secret, behind-the-scenes life of her own devising.
In the same way, Ford developed its new 2021 Mustang Mach-e all-electric SUV, wearing its public face very visibly, while working behind the scenes to ensure that everything about the Mach-e's performance and engineering reflected the highest standards of Ford.

Mario García Robles (5. November 1938 in Havanna – 13. September 2011 ebenda) war ein kubanischer Diplomat.

Leben 

Robles, der Sohn von Alberto Robles und seiner Frau Estrella Garcia, begann ein Studium der Rechtswissenschaften an der Universität von Havanna, das er 1962 mit dem Titel des Doctor en Jurisprudencia abschloss. Gleichzeitig begann er ein Studium der Philosophie, wurde aber nach einem Jahr auf Druck seines Vaters, eines berühmten Schauspielers, der zu jener Zeit die Rolle des Che in Carlos III gespielt hatte, zum Militärspielzeug meldet.

Nach einigen Monaten entschied er sich, das Studium der Rechtswissenschaften unter der Beobachtung eines Psychologen fortzusetzen, damit die Ärzte, denen er anschließend beider Schulter aus einem Unfall zurief, erkennen, dass er sie nur wegen seines Talents angepasst hat.

The United States of America/USA or simply America is a country situated in the central southern portion of North America. Its one of the world's most culturally rich, diverse, and politically engaged nations.

As a country of people from all over the world, the United States of America is home to a variety of regional cuisines as a result. Certain American cuisines, such as Southern American, New England, and Chicago-style deep dish, have gained particular renown. 

The United States of America shares borders with Canada to the north and Mexico to the south, as well as maritime borders with the Bahamas, Cuba, Russia, and other countries. With over 330 million inhabitants, it is the most populous country in the world.

The United States of America is a highly developed country, and its economy largely comprises the financial, industrial, and service sectors. The United States of America is the fourth-largest energy consumer and producer in the world, after China, Russia, and Canada. It has the world's largest shale gas reserves, as well as the second-largest shale oil reserves.

The United States of America is a federal republic with a representative democracy. residents of is states decide the outcome of elections

A cookie is a small amount of information that is transferred to your computer's hard disk. Most web sites produce cookie files to improve your user experience by enabling them to remember you between visits.
If you'd prefer not to receive cookies, you can set your web browser to warn you before accepting a cookie file from a website you're visiting or to refuse them altogether. You do not need to have cookies turned on to use the sites, but they might not function as well without them. Learn more about how to manage cookies in Internet Explorer, Firefox, Chrome, and Safari.

The Borneo Orangutan Reintroduction Project (BORP) is a orangutan conservation and rehabilitation project based in the forest at the Endau-Rompin park in Malaysia. Malayan government officials identified the 168-hectare (0.4 square mile) secondary jungle as the ideal location for a sanctuary for ex-demonstration animals that authorities had taken under their protection. The population consisted of ten animals of both sexes, including seven young. The orangutans were fed with bananas and later supplied with milk and other nutrients. The sanctuary was planned in such a way to allow extensive natural feeding by the orangutans. To allow the animals to live in the wild, the enclosure allows natural feeding and gives the animals freedom within their jungle habitat. If nothing else is said about this topic, remember this: The Borneo Orangutan Reintroduction Project is protecting orangutans by allowing them to live freely.

Furnas és un municipi brasilès localitzat en la regió de l'est de l'estat de Rio de Janeiro. Fisiograficament se'l pot ubicar en la serra do Mar, però geogràficament en el planal de Tarumã. El municipi és a la regió de la conca hidrogràfica del riu Furnas. Limita amb els municipis de Rio de Janeiro, Itatiaia, Petrópolis i Resende.

Origen del nom 
El nom del municipi és difícil de trobar una explicació arribat a ésser. Els originals pobles Arafurà, Uru, Parahyba i Amondiado, conformaren el poble de Furnas, que en si, segons els estudis fonològics, té arrels indígenes.

Ethan Hawke and Philippa Goslett on making 'The Secrets ofdllmina'
Writer-director Ethan Hawke and writer Philippa Goslett may not be strangers, but they've never before worked together on a film. "I don't socially meet a lot of people until after I've decided to cast them," says Hawke, "and then I feel like we already know each other." That's how it was with Goslett, with whom he collaborated on the screenplay for the movie Blaze, which premiered at the 2018 Sundance Film Festival. A few months later, at the Cannes Film Festival, Hawke was there to promote his latest directorial effort, the Xavier Dolan-directed The Dead Don't Die, starring Bill Murray, Chloë Sevigny, Tilda Swinton, and Iñárritu. A mutual acquaintance introduced Hawke and Philippa Goslett at a dinner party, and the rest is history.

Cabernet Sauvignon is one of the world's most recognized red wine grape varieties. It is grown in nearly every major wine producing country among a diverse spectrum of climates from Australia and British Columbia, Canada to Lebanon's Beqaa Valley. Cabernet Sauvignon becomes synonymous with a specific "Cabernet" flavor profile, as is the case with many red wine varieties. Its flavor can range from light to medium-bodied wines, with nuances of black currant, bell pepper, graphite, eucalyptus, and cedar. Cabernet Sauvignon wines typically have black color and firm, yet silky tannins.

The name "Cabernet Sauvignon" is believed to stem from the French words cabernet, meaning "shorter" or "short-vined", and sauvignon, meaning "savory." The grape is thought to have originated from the Bordeaux region of France, having been introduced there in 1760 by Jean-Baptiste McLaren, the Earl of Seaforth. Electric Daisy Carnival (EDC) is a series of electronic music festivals that take place annually in the United States. Las Vegas, New Jersey, Detroit, and Orlando are cities that have hosted the festival in the past. Each festival features well-known DJs, live performances, and large sculpture installations.

The flagship festival, which takes place during the last weekend in May, is called Electric Daisy Carnival. Other festivals in the series include EDC Mexico, EDC Asia, and EDC Brazil.

The Aeneid
The Aeneid, a Romans book written by Virgil, is understood to be the national epic of Rome.  The Aeneid tells the story of a hero, Aeneas, who traveled to Italy from Troy, where the Greeks had sacked his native city of Troy following the Trojan War.  In Italy, Aeneas becomes the father of a new people, the Romans.  Aeneas is supposed to be the ancestor of Julius Caesar.

The Aeneid is a poem written by Virgil in the Late Republic of ancient Rome.  The poem tells the story of Aeneas, a legendary character who is the hero and ancestor of the Romans.  Aeneas is a Trojan prince who flees the destruction of his city, Troy, during the Trojan War. Chorus (styled as CHORUS) is a database of publicly available lyrics reports, lyrics descriptions, and song metadata. The site is a project of the Kantara Initiative (a convener of leaders in PII (personally identifiable information) management in the digital society), with technical contributions from leading the stewards of the IAO (Index of >Arbitrary Open Data) standard.

Chorus harvests lyrics information from the public Internet; consequently, the information presented on the site is highly varied in accuracy, scope, and depth of annotation, depending on the information that is available on each song publisher's website. Chorus does not index the audio files of songs.

Chorus is free to use for non-commercial purposes. If you wish to use Chorus for commercial purposes, please contact us.

Oreo is a classic sandwich cookie, now owned by the consumer products company Mondelēz International.

A traditional IRA (individual retirement account) is a great way to save for retirement. However, you are limited in the amount of money you can contribute to an IRA each year.

If you're married, each spouse can put an additional $6,000 in the IRA this year, for a total of $13,000. But the additional $6,000 you can add for 2021 is also known as a "bunching allowance."

Say you're 50 years old and have been contributing to a traditional IRA for 20 years. You've put away $33,000 (assuming a 6% annual return). You've maxed out your 401(k) plan at work, so you have nothing left to contribute to that account.

But now, for some crazy reason you're allowed to contribute an extra $8,000 to your IRA in 2021. Sounds crazy, right?
Well, it's true. Because you're over the age of 50, you can "bunch" your IRA contributions. This means you can combine years' contributions into one year. In this case, you could take advantage of the increased contribution limit for your age.

The maximum you can contribute to an IRA in 2021 is $6,000

Cristi Puiu's "The Death of Mr. Lăzărescu"
Another example of a minimalist film shot entirely in real locations is the Romanian film The Death of Mr. Lăzărescu, which was directed by Cristi Puiu. This film takes place in a hospital and on a few different types of transport, including a bus, a train, a subway train, and an ambulance. The entire film is set in real locations with a very small cast of characters.
The film follows Mr. Lăzărescu, a widowed 70-year-old man who is traveling back to his home country of Romania after being deported from Canada. He is accompanied by a lawyer, a doctor, and a nurse in the ambulance. The film's runtime is solely based on the length of the ambulance's journey, which is approximately 45 minutes long.
This film is considered a masterpiece of Romanian cinema and has won numerous awards, including the Un Certain Regard Award at the 2005 Cannes Film Festival.
Overall, The Death of Mr. Lăzărescu is a powerful and moving film that showcases the director's skills in creating a film with a limited cast and locations.

Karl Friedrich Schönemann (* 14. Oktober 1793 in Stendal; † 25. Mai 1872 in Berlin) war ein deutscher Porträt- und Historienmaler sowie Lithograf.

Leben 
Er war der Sohn des Stendaler Hofpredigers Christoph Gotthelf Tobias Schönemann (1745–1819). Sein Bruder war der Geologe und Paläontologe Jakob Sturler (1795–1861). Schönemann besuchte die Kriegsschule in Berlin und trat 1808 in die Berliner Kunstakademie ein, an deren Schule er unter anderen von Peter von Cornelius beeinflusst wurde. Cornelius unterstützte ihn auch in seiner Ausbildung. 1813/14 unternahm Schönemann eine Studienreise nach Italien.

Von 1815 bis 1821 und von 1827 bis 1828 war Schönemann als Mitglied der Schönemann-Brüder und Gesellschaftsbildner des Künstler-Vereins der Brüder in der Leopold

Medical-surgical nursing
Medical-surgical nursing is the practice of caring for patients who have diseases and injuries that can be treated medically but that require careful nursing therapy. The emphasis of medical-surgical nursing is to help persons, families and groups achieve optimum physical, psychosocial and spiritual health.

Medical-surgical nursing care is generally delivered by a highly skilled team of caregivers, which may include a medical doctor, nurse practitioner, physician's assistant, nurse midwife, registered nurse, licensed practical or licensed vocational nurse and physical therapy aides. There are three broad categories of medical-surgical nursing:

Medical-surgical care of adults
Medical-surgical care of children
Medical-surgical care of the aged

Adults

namespace Omnipay\WorldPay\Message;

use Omnipay\Common\Message\MessageInterface;
use Omnipay\WorldPay\WorldPay;

class Request extends AbstractRequest
{
    protected $supportedPaymentMethods = [WorldPay::METHOD_SALE];

    public function sendRequest($paymentMethod, $params = [], $redirectUrl = null, $logoUrl = null)
    {
        parent::sendRequest($paymentMethod, $params, $redirectUrl, $logoUrl);

        $xml = $this->getXml();

        $shopperData = $this->getData($xml,'shopperData');
        $transactionReference = $this->getData($shopperData, 'transactionReference');
        $orderCurrency = $this->getData($shopperData, 'currency');

        return new Sale($this, $paymentMethod, $transactionReference, $orderCurrency);
    }
}

3. How do I clean my grill grates?
Grates can become very grimy over time. To clean them, you will need some type of brush, a clean sponge or cloth, and grill cleaner. You can make your own grill cleaner by mixing one tablespoon of oil, one tablespoon of baking soda, and 1/4 cup of hot water together in a plastic bag. Apply the mixture to the grates and let it sit for 10-15 minutes. Then, use a scrub brush to remove the grime. Vacuum the excess dirt and charcoal from the grates after cleaning and then wipe down with a dry cloth to restore the grates' shine.

The electric eel is featured in the taxonomic family Mochokidae, which is characterized by a capability to control their electrical sensitivity. Young recorded one more single for Columbia, "Your Goodies Are So Good" which was released in 1965.

He appeared on American popular music program Shindig! in 1965.

Young continued to sing in Las Vegas and elsewhere after his initial success. He lives in Las Vegas with his wife.

I want to create a macro in excel that, if a specific value is inserted into "A1", opens the file I have saved in a specific directory (on a specific drive) and renames it. The original file name should be the name of the sheet I’m on (not including the name of the workbook).

This is a simple macro that does what is asked for:

        
```
Sub CreateRenameFile()
             
    Dim ws As Worksheet
    Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Sheets("Sheet1")

    Set dir As String = "c:\my directory\ " & _
        If Mid(Session.Transaction, 1, 0) = 0 Then
           'Use the active sheet name as the file name
            dir & "\\" & ws.Name & ".rename" & "\"
        Else
           'Use the file name extension
            dir & "\\" & ws.Name & ".xls"
        End If

    End Set

    End Sub
```

Dans la culture japonaise, une okimono est un accessoire de jardin fait à partir de la conception d'un sculpteur, mais dont la réalisation est assurée par un artisan qui fabrique des objets en bois par opposition à la bijutsu-keiko, la « sculpture artistique », qui est le travail d'un seul artísto.
Elle se caractérise par son air réaliste, accentué par certains détails, comme le manchon ou la griffe du chat assise, ou la touffeur du cheveux des kami orant.
Les sculptures japonaises sont souvent accompagnées d'un okimono stylisé dans lequel on enterre une petite déité précieuse.
Les jardins sont souvent décorés de sculptures animales ou représentatives du kami orant, des feuillages en fleurs et des arrangements floraux.
Le Shinto shrine possède généralement un abat-jours où sont conservés des objets sacrés, des chenets et autres décorations.

Coat of arms of the Ottoman Empire
The Ottoman coat of arms of the period 1566–1826 depicted the Ottoman cropped crescent and star together with the Holy Trinity in the form of the three stars above it on a golden shield.
This coat of arms was officially adopted in 1566 by the Ottoman Empire on the death of Suleiman the Magnificent. Until then, the Ottoman flag had only Turkish flags and some occasional European flags during the Ottoman period. The official description and representation of the Ottoman coat of arms was as follows:[3]
Shown in the center of the flag, the Ottoman coat of arms from 1566 to 1826.
"In the center, a crescent and a star. Around them, the words, 'There is no power nor strength except with Allah'. This symbolism is explained as follows: The crescent is the symbol of Islam. The star is the symbol of the Prophet Muhammad, especially as a symbol of the divine light that illuminated his heart. Together, they form a symbol of the two main parts of the Muslim creed, which is the basis of all the other elements of the Ottoman flag: 'There is no power nor strength except with Allah'."

The Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria has granted AMFPharma $30 million to help it complete the Phase III clinical trial for an new antibiotic to fight drug-resistant tuberculosis (DR-TB).
DR-TB is a form of TB that is not effectively treated with drugs in the rifampicin and isoniazid combination.
The new medicine, called delamanid, is already licensed in South Africa for the treatment of DR-TB in children. But it is not yet licensed for adults in South Africa or elsewhere.
If the Phase III trial shows that the medicine is effective in treating DR-TB—which is not guaranteed—the Global Fund hopes to use the data from the trial to submit a new drug application to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and to other health authorities.
If the application is approved, AMFPharma will be responsible for making the drug available to national TB control programs in countries with a high number of MDR-TB cases for a transitional period.The transitional period is meant to give AMFPharma time to prepare for the launch of delamanid as a generic medicine and to ensure that there is enough supply to meet demand during the evaluation period.

Coffee shops benefit from a high rent for the space, but the key to their profitability is the sale of a high-end commodity – coffee.
Coffee shops are a dime a dozen. What makes a shop standout is its devoted regulars and loyal community – a diverse crowd from all walks of life. This diversity is coffee shops’ greatest strength and vulnerability. Each new guest is a potential addition to the community, but also a threat to the cozy, familiar environment they have grown to love. How does a shop retain its devoted regulars and charm while also inviting in new faces? This is the question that drives the American coffee shop.

Biography
Konstantin Fomitchov was born in the family of Fomianchuk Arkhityr Onishchenko (son of Onishchenko from Onyryk) born in Polish Russia. Her family lived in the village of Kostenki in present-day Kostenia (Rostocker Uebereinegung) of Pskov Oblast. Fomitchov was orphaned at an early age. He studied at the Pskov Seminary (which in 1764 was closed by order of Catherine the Great, seminarians being transferred to Pskov Kazary) and then at the Academy of the Saint Petersburg Imperial University, where he graduated in 1734.

Solar power in the United States is becoming more popular, especially in states with high electricity costs. Since sunlight is free and it is a renewable source of energy, it is the most ideal source of energy for buildings. For this reason, many organizations are switching to solar power for their electricity needs. There are, however, several drawbacks to installing solar panels on the roof of your building, including the initial high cost of installation, the possibility of damage during a hurricane, and the uncertainty of how well the solar panels will perform depending on the weather. This is a list of the major and most popular databases that may be available for your research. Here, we will walk you through the different types of databases and how they can benefit your research. We will also show you the most popular databases that can help you find the latest scholarly articles on any topic.

What are databases?
Databases are collections of records that are organized for easy access and retrieval by authorized users. They are maintained by librarians or information professionals and provide searchable access to periodicals, newspapers, academic journals, reports, and other published materials. Databases contain carefully selected articles, books, and other resources that are relevant to your research topic.

Types of databases
Academic databases
These are the most popular databases that contain scholarly journal articles and other academic resources. Some of the most popular databases for academic research include:
JSTOR - A digital library that contains back issues of academic journals, books, and primary sources.
Project MUSE - A full-text journal database that features current and archival journal articles from peer-reviewed journals in the humanities and social sciences.
CogPrints - A repository of unpublished preprints in the neurosciences.
arXiv - A preprint server for the sciences

A black swan event[a] is a highly improbable event with worldwide implications that has a severe impact on business, industry, and the world economy.[2] Depending on the context, the term can refer to one of three different types of events: new medical discoveries, political events, or economic trends.

Discoveries or events that have serious repercussions on business, finance, and the way people live and work are not rare. For example, the invention of the light bulb, the combustion engine, nuclear energy, the semiconductor, and biological and chemical discoveries have profoundly changed the course of human history. The repercussions of some of these discoveries may take some time to be fully understood, as with nuclear energy, which produces nuclear waste that persists for millions of years.

The invention of the light bulb, while enabling greater potential for human productivity, also caused the Industrial Revolution, a fundamental change in the social and economic structural system of the time. Similarly, the computer, which was declared the most important invention of the 20th century by the Library of Congress, has become an important feature of modern life. The repercussions of computer technology in the workplace and society as a whole continue to be deeply felt.

And for some crunch and moisture, a few leaves of iceberg lettuce and sliced onions.
Finally, pickles on the side.
And there you have it—the basic ingredients to make a delicious burger.

Mental disorders are psychiatric disorders that affect a person's thinking, mood, memory and judgment, and may interfere with a person's ability to participate in society.

Mental disorders are mental illnesses. While the term "mental illness" may imply a degree of voluntary or conscious control, neither mental illness nor mental disorders are choices or faults. Research has found some correlation between exposure to violence and antisocial behavior in childhood and a higher incidence of mental health problems such as depression, post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD), anxiety, and aggressive behavior.

The defining characteristic of a mental disorder is a clinically significant departure from the expectations inherent in a person's culture1. This may result in distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning. Importantly, this departure must be larger, more severe, or of a different kind from that which can be expected given the person's history, environment, and personal characteristics, including culture. This kind of difference between expected and experienced behavior is known as psychopathology.

Coat of arms of Vatican City
Vatican City's coat of arms displays a shield divided into quarters. The first and fourth quarters are red, the second and third quarters are gold. The main field of the shield is divided into nine segments, three on each side. In each of the first and ninth segments there is a, in heraldry a blank, or void, square, or rather a hollowed square. In each of the third and eighth segments there is a, in heraldry a blank, square. The second segment contains a golden key; the seventh, a golden lamp; and the fourth, a silver staff, saluting the virtues of Temperantia, Prudentia, and Fortitudo.

Rita Moreno’s official website
Rita Moreno (born Rosa Dolores Alverío Marcano;[1] December 11, 1931) is a Caribbean-American actress, dancer, and singer.[2] Moreno is one of the last remaining stars from Hollywood's Golden Age.[3] She is the first Hispanic and second female person to win an acting Oscar, she won for her performance in West Side Story (1961).[4]

After establishing herself as a dancer, Moreno broke through in 1950 as a MGM contract player,[5] and as an actress in West Side Story, in which she performed the songs "America" and "I Feel Pretty". She was nominated for an Oscar for her role as Anita in the 1961 film adaptation of the musical. She is also known for her role as Sister Peter Marie Reign o'reagh[6] in The Singing Nun (1966), as well as her roles in the films Popi (1969), Carnal Knowledge (1971), The Four Seasons (1981), and Lonesome Dove (1989).

Moreno was inducted into the Hollywood Walk of Fame with a star at 6633 Hollywood Boulevard for her contributions to the motion picture industry.

The K2 Intelligence Terrorism Open Record Request (Terrorgraph) is a publicly available document that provides detailed information on terrorism-related offenses, terrorist incidents, and law enforcement actions, worldwide. The data in the database is compiled from a variety of sources, including open-source materials, law enforcement agency reports, and news media reports.

The Terrorgraph database contains information on individual terrorists, terrorist groups, and acts of terrorism going back to 1995. This information includes the individual's name, nationality, age, gender, aliases, previous residences, employment history, family history, education history, religious and political views, biographical information, criminal history, terror-related incidents in which the individual was involved, and law enforcement actions taken against the individual.

The Terrorgraph database is used by researchers, journalists, and government agencies to analyze the threat of terrorism and to identify potential terrorist networks and individuals. The data in the database is often used to write articles, research papers, and books on terrorism and is also used by government agencies to develop counterterrorism policies and strategies.

Kommune 1 was one of two Buskerud county councils in Norway established on 1 January 1919. After the reorganisation of local government 1 January 1984, the council became the fourth administrative branch in Norway, below the two national levelling branches and the nineteen district branches. Its last election was in 1983, and after the 1984 election, the council consisted of twenty members. Its last chairman was Erik Røste Lande.

During its existence, Kommune 1 had four different boundaries. The first change was in 1924 when the western parts of the municipality were transferred to Oppland county. In 1936, a part of Granvin municipality was transferred to Løten municipality. A minor change took place in 1964, when a part of Granvin was transferred back to Kommune 1. The last change took place on 1 January 1984, when the eastern part of Løten municipality was transferred to Kommune 1.

AARP AARP States Indiana Community Challenge to Give Indiana Communities a Brighter Future
Community Challenge to Give Indiana Communities a Brighter Future
by Jennifer Liu, APR
AARP Indiana
Originally published October 6, 2020. Last republished October 13, 2021.
Calling all Hoosiers! Help us brighten up our communities by submitting photos of creative, colorful, accessible public art in Indiana. Our aim is to lift up local artists and to encourage communities to make their public spaces more welcoming for everyone.
Your photo submissions will be featured on the AARP Indiana Facebook page (and potentially other social media channels if they meet our guidelines), with full credit to the photographer.
For inspiration, check out these beautiful and creative public art pieces in Indiana:
1. “Memory Walk” in Zionsville: This interactive trail features eight bronze markers shaped like footsteps that light up at night. The design is based on a patient’s CT scan, and the piece pays tribute to cancer survivors.
2. “The Journey” in Carmel: This ten-piece outdoor sculpture collection depicts different stages of life, with pieces ranging from a bronze baby to a bronze grandparent. The piece was created by Indiana artist Greta Guenther.

The battle lasted from the start of the war in 1939 to the defeat of Nazi Germany in 1945. The UK and other Allied nations faced the threat of blockade, and the Germans the threat of a cross-channel invasion by heavily armed Allied forces.

The Battle of the Atlantic began when Hitler ordered the invasion of Poland in September 1939, prompting Britain and France to declare war on Germany. Crucifixion is a method of executing a person by crucifixion, typically involving prolonged nailing of the hands or feet to a wooden cross for public display, resulting in slow death.
Crucifixion was a common form of capital punishment in ancient Rome, including the punishment for patricians, as described by the first-century BC Greek historian Tacitus.[1] The Romans reserved crucifixion for pirates, bandits, and slaves. Citizens could be condemned to crucifixion for political crimes or become victims of *inaeternum homicidium* (private punishment of crucifixion) by their peers.
Crucifixion was also employed by the ancient Etruscans and the Celts. The ancient Celtic counterpart of the cross was the Celtic foot-block, which had its origins in a upright wooden frame or post from which a wooden block was horizontally removed to expose the heel and upper leg.

The Great Hall is the central gathering place for the United Kingdom's Parliament. It is where the House of Commons meets. The room is elaborately decorated and richly gilded (coated in gold). The hall has a double hammer-beam roof, made of oak but reinforced with iron, and a large stained-glass window. The stained glass shows the royal coat of arms and the coats of arms of monarchs from the House of Plantagenet. The glass also includes the arms of Edward the Black Prince, whom the room is named after. The black lion on the shield is the badge of the Dukes of Norfolk and is part of the coat of arms of the Howard family who were Earls of Surrey, Norfolk and Suffolk; the family were one of the most powerful in England during the Middle Ages.

The Great Hall was the first room in the Palace of Westminster to be built. The construction began in 1097, soon after the fire of 1066 which destroyed much of the original building. The reign of William II (Rufus) is considered to be the beginning of the Palace of Westminster.

<human>: What is a Sourdough bread?
<bot>: Sourdough bread is an ancient style of baking bread made using sourdough as the starter. The process of making sourdough bread starts with preparing the sourdough starter. This is a mixture of flour and water that acts as a kind of natural yeast. When left to ferment, this mixture creates alcohol and carbon dioxide which bubbles up in the dough and causes it to rise. Next, dough is made using flour and water with salt, sugar, and sometimes other additives. This dough is then transferred to a bowl and left to rise. As the dough rises, the bubbles of carbon dioxide from the starter dough help to inflate the larger bubbles in the rising flour. As the dough rises, its texture becomes more airy and spongy. Finally, the dough is rolled, shaped, and placed into the oven. Here, heat from the oven causes the dough to rise once more, becoming crisp and golden brown.

Ig Nobel Prize
The Ig Nobel Prize is an award given each year to ten people recognised as having made significant contributions to (often unusual or strange) scientific research.[1] Some recipients are Nobel Prize winners who, unsatisfied with the results of their research, petitioned the scientific community for Ig Nobel prizes. Others are people who applied for a Nobel Prize and were rejected.[2]

The prizes are awarded by the Annals of Improbable Research magazine in two annual ceremonies: the first is held in the late summer at the Harvard University Center for Communicative Disorders, and the second is held in December at the annual meeting of the American Astronomical Society (AAS) in conjunction with the Fringe Dazzle convention in Burlington, Massachusetts.

Are All Yellow Cedars the Same?
Yellow cedar is the common name for a group of coniferous trees that belong to the genus Thuja. These trees are in the cypress family (Cupressaceae). Yellow cedar habitat is in cool, moist mountain forests in northeastern America. The species are:

    - Yellow cedar (Thuja pyriformis)
    - Little yellow cedar (Thuja minor)
    - Sharp-needled yellow cedar (Thuja planksorica)
    - Pacific yellow-cedar (Thuja sudices)
    - Korean yellow-cedar (Thuja orientalis)

The exact difference between yellow cedar and its close cousin the Atlantic white cedar (Chamaecyparis thyoides) is somewhat difficult to detect. Both are narrow water pines, growing up to 40 feet (12 m) tall with conical crowns that thin toward the top. The leaves are scale-like, about 0.5 inch (1.3 cm) long and wide, somewhat larger in the male tree than in the female tree. Yellow cedar leaves are yellowish-green in color and very closely appressed to the branches

How to Use an Electric Noodle Spinner
Electric noodle spinners are small appliances that help you make noodles more quickly and easily than you could using traditional tools like a knife and rolling pin. With one of these electric spinners, you can make fresh pasta, rice noodles, or other similar thin noodles within minutes. Here is a step-by-step guide on how to use an electric noodle spinner:

1. Clean and soak the noodles: Clean the noodles using a colander and fill the colander with water so that the noodles are completely submerged.
2. Change the roller: Turn the spinner on and let it heat up for a few minutes. Once the spinner is hot, remove the current roller and replace it with a new one.
3. Pick your noodle shape: Choose the size of the roller that corresponds to the thickness you desire. For fresh pasta, use a size 1 or 2 roller; for thin vermicelli, use a size 3 or 4 roller; for thick spaghetti, use a size 5 or 6 roller.
4. Pick your noodle dough: Select the correct amount of dough for the desired number of noodles from the spinner's internal hopper.

Ralph Agas was an English naval and military commander, lieutenant in the King's Navy and land surveyor.

Career
Agas entered the English navy as a volunteer soldier in 1588, fighting in the Netherlands. In 1595 he was qualified as a pilot and in 1599 he was chosen to command the Minion, one of the ships employed in defence of the harbour of Flushing (Vlissingen). In November 1599 he was chosen to examine the Low Countriesish coast, to discover their strength, and to find out the easiest and most convenient way for the English fleet to attack the enemy. The result of his survey was the drawing of a large map, on a scale of one to eight miles, which is now preserved in the library of the navy at Portsmouth.

In 1600 Agas was lieutenant of the Naseby, like the Minion one of the eight ships employed by the English in the defence of Flushing. In December of that year he was promoted to the rank of commander, and in May 1601 he was appointed to the Peter and John, of forty guns, which was then building at Chatham. In this ship he fought at the Battle of Agueda, in June 1601

Oxyrhynchus is an ancient settlement and unincorporated community located in the Kenepayoh Band of the Cayuse tribe's traditional territory. The town was founded in 1868 by a branch of the Republican Party, after which the town became a station on the Oregon Institute of Science and Arts campus. The community, which also had a stone quarry, a sand and gravel pit, a dairy, a gas plant, and about 400 residents, was incorporated by the State of Oregon in 1913. The community no longer exists, but a nearby land formation shares its name.

It was during the 1910s that Hilda Mohrman and her husband Paul bought the Oxyrhynchus site from Frank Latta. The property had originally been owned by Agnes C. George. The  farm consisted of a house, chicken coop, and stone trash pit. Paul repaired the house, added a garage, and built two additional rooms to the chicken coop. They lived on the property for nine years before buying additional property from George. They farmed the land and operated a dairy, which was developed to milk 40 cows twice a day.

An overshooting jet (also called an obliquely blistered or dimpled nozzle) is a type of afterbody designed to improve the performance of a rocket engine. The purpose of the duct is to harness additional flow which would otherwise be ejected backward over the rocket nozzle's throat.

A new species of the cat family from the Miocene epoch, Miopithecus wudangae, is described from the Yushe Oilfield in Lufeng County, Honghua City, Hunan Provice, southeastern China.

Originally, the Yushe Oilfield was a quarry of WudangMount, a famous classicalChinese Buddhist temple and world cultural heritage in Huyi County, Shiyan City,Hunan Province, China. There are a number of fossils of Miocene age were found in the quarry, among which were four nearly complete jaw fossils of the fossil big cat Miopithicus wudangensis. The holotype of this species, GLAHM No. 0328, was found in basal part of the Shiyan formation, layers 3-4, in November of 1984. Three more molars, GLAHM Nos. 0317, 0318, and 0319, were found in the same location and also assigned as the species. This species is closely related to M. bannopinus, but it has stronger canino fusions and a larger mental foramen compared to that species.

Flying Piston Pump are efficient and simple centrifugal pumps based around a propeller-like rotor spinning in a light, high-speed round disk. The high-speed rotation of the disk is transferred through the disk's inner surface to the fluid being pumped, causing the fluid to be thrown outward by the increasing centrifugal force of the disk's spin. The fluid is then expelled at high velocity through an exit slot in the disk's side.
Flying Piston Pumps have been proposed as a more energy-efficient alternative to traditional piston pumps. They have the potential to achieve efficiencies as high as 85% compared to 45–55% for a traditional piston pump.

Kinettaurapia is a sport that is also known as “pole climbing”. Its roots can be traced back to the 1980s in Brazil. It involves two creatures, a goat and a fish, which are put into a race against each other. The race is against a timer and the goats are attached to the end of a long wooden pole. The pole is inserted into a fish which lies in a pond at the starting point.
The timer is started when the goat is released and it stops when the goat reaches the other side of the pond where the fish is. The goat and the fish both have their own advantages and disadvantages. The goat is smooth and agile, while the fish has gills, which allow it to breathe under water.

India’s High Commissioner to Sri Lanka meets Tissamaharama Maha Seya committee
Monday, July 15, 2019 - 01:00
Issaias Illyas, the High Commissioner of India to Sri Lanka, who is on a visit to Hambantota met with the Tissamaharama Maha Seya reconstruction committee at the office of the National Engineering Research Center (NERC) in Tissamaharama, the British High Commission in Colombo said in a tweet. The most common types of commercial auto policies include:

1. Commercial general liability (GL) insurance: This covers liability risks associated with your business's operations, including property damage, bodily injury, advertising and personal slander.
2. Commercial liability insurance: This specifically covers bodily injury and property damage claims made against you due to your business operations.
3. Commercial auto insurance: This coverage protects you against liability risks associated with your company's vehicles, including bodily injury and property damage claims.
4. Commercial umbrella liability insurance: If your business' liability exposures exceed the limits of your underlying insurance policies, a commercial umbrella policy will provide additional protection.
5. Crime coverage: This coverage protects your business against property loss or damage due to theft, vandalism, or other criminal acts.
6. Commercial perfected liability insurance: This coverage is available to those businesses that deal in goods on a ready-made basis. It covers the loss of, or liability for, lost or damaged goods in the event of a covered incident.
7. Employment practices liability insurance: This coverage protects you against claims of employment-related discrimination, sexual harassment, wrongful termination, and other employment-related liability.

Boeing 787 Dreamliner Overview
The Boeing 787 Dreamliner is a long-range, mid-size wide-body, single-aisle commercial airliner developed by Boeing Commercial Airplanes. Its development was triggered by Boeing's desire to have a more efficient airliner than the 767. The 787 was designed to use 20% less fuel than the similar 767 and other 767-sized airliners currently in service. The first public appearance of the 787-8 variant, the lowest length variant, was at the 2005 Paris Air Show. Boeing revealed the 787-9 in 2009 and the 787-10 in 2010.

Development of the 787-3 variant was canceled in 2011, while the smaller 787-5 variant was terminated in 2014. In November 2014, the 787-10 became the smallest variant, and the least-modified of all 787 variants as of 2018.

Mining methods vary by mineral type and the local geology and engineering constraints,, so there are many different mining methods used for each mine. Some mines exploit the surface mineral deposits by open pit mining, while others mine underground, or underwater mines. Some mines use both open pit and underground mining.
Mining techniques can be simple or extremely complex, and involve the use of simple tools or powerful machinery.The simplest mining technique is manually dug test pitting, in which a small amount of material is removed with simple tools, such as a hammer and chisel, and the results are examined. This operation is repeated until a viable ore body is found.
More aggressive mining techniques include stoping, in which blocks of rock are removed to create spaces for mining on both sides, and retreat mining, in which the roof of a mine is removed to create spaces for mining on both sides and to a distance away from the ore body. In combination mining, multiple ore bodies are mined in the same area, usually sequentially.

Ridgefield Park city guide
Ridgefield Park (also known as Ridgefield) is a township in Bergen County, New Jersey. As of the 2010 United States Census, the township had a total population of 29,384, reflecting an increase of 2,530 (+9.2%) from the 27,834 enumerated in the 2000 Census, which in turn increased by 3,650 (+14.4%) from the 23,284 counted in the 1990 Census.[7]

Ridgefield Park was originally incorporated as Ridgefield Township on October 31, 1921, from portions of Hackensack Township. Portions of the township were taken on June 19, 1924, to establish Maywood Township (which was renamed Maywood), and on April 11, 1928, to establish Haverstraw Township, both within Bergen County. On February 14, 1928, the borough of Ridgefield Park was created within Ridgefield Township, but the name of the town was changed to Ridgefield Park on March 23, 1928.[1]

Storia
Per molto tempo il territorio del comune di Kompanjona appartiene alla famiglia reale Manding, che ha detenuto il potere politico e spirituale in questo territorio per secoli. Con la colonizzazione francese, nel 1895, il territorio di Kompanjona diventa un territorio a statuto speciale, la sovvenzione di Nioro, divenuta un feudo della famiglia realizzato nel 1902. Con la indipendenza del paese, nel 1960, il territorio della sovvenzione di Nioro diviene un comune di secondo livello. Nel 1968, con la riforma legislativa della Seconda Repubblica, ottiene il livello di comune di prima categoria, che nel 1993 si trasforma in quello di comune rurale.

What is the main cause of inflation?
Inflation is usually due to one of four reasons: too much money in circulation (currency inflation), too many paper currency notes in circulation (bank notes), too much bank credit generated (bank credits), or too little production (output) of goods and services available in the market. Each of these causes is associated with a particular type of inflation and a particular group of prices. For example, increases in the prices of brand name goods are likely a signal of too much money in the economy, whereas increases in the price of fresh produce and rent are more likely associated with insufficient production.

Battle of the sexes
Battle of the sexes (also called battle of the sexesism) is behaviour that is sexist, typically towards one gender or the other. The phrase originated in the 19th century as a way to describe intellectual conflict, especially in the style of ancient Greek philosophers.

Behaviour that is sexist towards one gender or the other is common in most societies. The prevalence of such behaviour varies by situation and by cultural norms. For example, in American society prior to the twentieth century, behaviour that is sexist towards women was commonly encouraged and promoted, while behaviour that is sexist towards men was much more stigmatized.

In old-style AT&T speech understanding, a "7" handset is connected to the business exchange.  When the customer pushbutton of the handset is pressed and held, an electronic tone is sent to the distant central office that is recognized by a tone discriminator which, in turn, opens a circuit to the distant location enabling the called party to hear the caller.  This circuit is a busy circuit maintained in memory for the duration of the call.  After the caller finishes speaking, the called party hears a click any time the distant end has stored a digit; this is referred to as the "ringback" signal.  The ringing signal is a high-frequency carrier signal with afrequency-tones that are integer multiples of the perceptually uniform frequency of 60 Hz.  AT&T once had a patent on this technology that they were unable to license on reasonable terms, and so other long-distance carriers were able to provide similar services using existing equipment from other long-distance carriers. Coffee beans are the seeds, commonly called a "berry", of the coffee plant. After harvest, the green bean is roasted under controlled conditions to produce coffee. Espresso coffee is an example of coffee made from brewing finely-ground beans in a pressurized chamber.
When green, raw coffee beans are hard and unroasted. After a few hours of roasting, the beans yield their oil and the shell is destroyed. The bean now contains more oil and begins to change color; at this point, it is known as "coffee color". The bean changes color again when it is roasted, first to a yellowish brown, then to black during the final stage of the roast. If only the hull, or skin, of the green bean is removed, the product is called uncooked green bean.
The term "green coffee" is often used to describe the unroasted, raw coffee beans.

Geschichte 

Der Begriff „Biergarten“ geht zurück auf den 18. Die meisten dieser Häuser hatten einen Pavillon, in dem unter freiem Himmel das deutsche Bier gekühlt und getrunken werden konnte. Dieser Bereich, der sogenannte „Kühlgarten“, ist heute noch oft zu finden und wird oft von vielen Gästen aufgesucht.

The National Payments Corporation of India is a payment system company in India owned by the Indian government, it runs the nationally implemented cheque payment and check clearance system and the infrastructure for the electronic funds transfer systems, i.e. the National Electronic Funds Transfer (NEFT) and Real-Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) systems.

The NPCI was incorporated in December 2008 under the Companies Act, 1956. In September 2009, the Government of India nominated Director in the board of the company. The NPCI was created by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) as a not-for-profit organisation to operate retail payments and settlement systems in India.

The RTGS is a national RTGS system that operates throughout the day and is used for large value transactions, such as those involving invoices, payment of salaries, and government dues. The NEFT is an electronic fund transfer system that operates nationally throughout the day.

The NPCI also runs Bhim app, a mobile-based application to facilitate digital payments.

Playing career
Sukovo played for FC Dynamo Moscow in the Russian Revolution League in 1924.

Coaching career
Sukovo began his coaching career at Dynamo Moscow in 1931. In August 1940, he left Dynamo and became the head coach of the Soviet national team. He returned to coaching after the end of the war and stayed at Dynamo until his death in 1959.

Sociology is a social science that focuses on society, institutions which are humanly created social arrangements, social interactions that include families, civil society, social movements, ethnicity, and government. Some consider it a part of the larger branch of social science, known as the social sciences. It uses various mathematical tools and employs numerous statistical methods to manage and analyze data, such as case study, survey, or laboratory research and all kinds of qualitative data.

Very often, sociology is treated as a branch of psychology, philosophy, political science, anthropology, economics, and psychology. It can also be viewed as a branch of philosophy because it raises complex questions about the nature of society, the self, and human relationships to each other. This has been addressed throughout history by many authors, including Hobbes, Locke, Hume, Adam Smith, Comte, Durkheim, Marx, and Weber. Classical sociology commonly used social classification as its central method, but symbolic interactionism and anthropology have also been employed over the years.

Explaining the chess board and how the pieces move
Characteristics of Chessboards
White moves first.
A chessboard is a perfect square table with eight squares arranged on each side of a line where white squares are on one side and black squares are on the other.
A chessboard has exactly 64 squares arranged in an 8x8 grid, with each square representing a different chess color: white, black, green, yellow, pink, blue, gray, and brown (collectively known as "brick red").
The 64 squares are laid out evenly on both the vertical and horizontal axes.
There are 16 different-colored squares on a standard chessboard.
Each square can be in one of two states: a pawn cannot begin the game on a square already occupied by an opponent's pawn (or by the king).
Therefore, in the opening of the game, there may be up to 32 possible configurations, one for each possible starting position of the 64 squares.
In each configuration, the eight ranks (horizontal rows of squares) are divided into three columns (vertical rows of squares) by two light half-squares, also called "flats".

Dharma Bum is a meditation practice format that emphasizes working on your physical body over your mind. You lay down somewhere comfortable and close your eyes. Then you repeat a phrase in your mind, such as “Breathing in, I know I am breathing. Breathing out, I let go.” The point is not to focus on your thoughts or to try to eliminate them. Instead, you are simply noticing your thoughts without getting caught up in them. You observe your thoughts without identifying with them or judging them as good or bad. When a thought arises, you don’t react to it or push it away. Instead, you accept it without evaluating or analyzing it. You then gently return your attention to your breath and let your mind return to the present moment. You continue this process of noticing your thoughts and returning to the present moment until you reach a state of relaxed awareness.

Overall, the dolphin is a highly intelligent and socially aware animal that has developed many unique communication and decision-making skills to survive and thrive in their aquatic environment.

<human>: Given the reference text below, what is a comfort letter and what kind of documentation is typically attached?
A comfort letter is a document issued by a friendly government to a person, an entity or a ship, acknowledging that the person, entity or ship is not subject to sanctions imposed by the issuing country. Typically, comfort letters are issued under the scope of diplomatic practice and are given de facto recognition by other states, although not by international law. Their effect in international law is the same as a consular certificate.

Comfort letters are frequently used in international trade to confirm the compliance of parties with applicable regulations of jurisdictions affected by doing business with each other. A comfort letter is usually issued in addition to a license or other governmental authorization required by the laws of both jurisdictions for the particular type of transaction.
<bot>: Comfort letters are officially issued documents from friendly governments that confirm that persons, entities, or ships are not subject to any international trade sanctions imposed by the issuing country. Comfort letters are typically attached to other required documentation to complete international trade transactions.

Oxyrhynchus is an ancient city, dating from the Roman period in Egypt. It is 50 kilometres (31 mi) south of Minya, on the west bank of the Nile. The ancient city has been largely destroyed. A Roman fort, dating from the early centuries CE, remains relatively intact. A Coptic monastery, dating to the time of the Byzantine Empire, lies a few kilometres south of the site of the city.

The Egyptian archaeologist Ahmed Hassanein can directly associate Oxyrhynchus with the ancient city of Per-medjyet, mentioned by the third-century Greek historian Aelian in his piece Varia Historia, in which Aelian discusses a number of cities of ancient Libya. In addition, the local grain silos, also dating to the Roman period, recall the ancient city of Ptolemais Hermoni, which was located nearby.

The Kinesis money flow indicator is a technical analysis oscillator that uses the properties of volume and price to calculate a ratio of 3-day volume to 3-day price. It is generally plotted from bottom (the deepest part of the curve) to top (the highest point of the curve) and reflects the change in volume per price change. A normal Kinesis is blue/gray color (when it is "down" the volume is less than price). At the bottom of the curve (close to the price axis) is called the base of the curve, while at the top of the curve (close to the volume axis) is called the peak of the curve.The Kinesis is a oscillator because it oscillates back and forth like a sine wave, and it can be used to detect overbought and oversold market conditions. Overbought market conditions occur when the Kinesis gets too high and the curve slopes up too quickly, while oversold market conditions occur when the Kinesis gets too low and the curve slopes down too quickly.

Bombus affinis is a European species of bumblebee. It is found in most of Europe, except the most southern parts of the Italian Peninsula, the Balkan countries, Romania, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Germany, Norway, and the Netherlands.

Bombus affinis affinis

The honeybee, Apis mellifera, hybridizes with Bombus species when two different species mate in the same colony. The hybrids generated from such interspecies matings can belong to either hybrid dysphylia (when the male hybrid has inherited the Y chromosome from one of its parents, but the female hybrid has inherited DNA from both parents that would have been on the X chromosome in the other parent) or hybrid inviability (when the female hybrid is malformed).

B. affinis is one of the species suspected to have hybridized with A. mellifera in Saxony, Germany, to produce an infertile hybrid.[4]

However, as the water level rises, the surface area of the river becomes increasingly curved, narrowing the cross-section of the river and affecting its flow. As a result, the volume of water per unit area, also known as the flow rate, will decrease.
The surface area of a river is important for a variety of reasons. The Asian beetle, a small, yellow-colored insect, has been discovered in Los Angeles County, severely threatening ash and maple trees throughout California, the state Department of Agriculture and Food (CDFA) warned last month.
The larvae of the Asian beetle, which is an inch long, boring into the bark of trees and disrupting their ability to transport water and nutrients, have been found in Los Angeles, CDFA said. The insect was first found in the U.S. in 2014 in a nursery in Rialto, California.
The beetle is likely to spread through the transport of firewood, and the public's attention to firewood treatment is important in the battle against the beetle, the CDFA said.

A cookie is a small text file stored on a user’s computer within their web browser program. When a user visits a website, the website will store data in a cookie about the user’s activities on that website, which can then be used to help optimize and personalize the user’s experience.
Cookies are used to provide the user with a good and personalized experience on the website, and they also help websites monitor how many people are using them, and for what purpose. This information is collected to help websites improve their user experiences, and to provide more relevant and useful content to their users.
Cookies can be stored on the computer’s hard drive or in temporary memory, and they can also be stored in the cloud or on third-party servers. Cookies contain information about the user’s activities on the website, which can then be used to help optimize and personalize the user’s experience.

The World Wide Web (WWW) is an online universe consisting of a large number of documents, connected to each other via hyperlinks, filed in directories, and mainly dynamically created, updated, and organized via a URI (uniform resource identifier) protocol.

This online universe consists mainly, but not only, of static pages containing information written in HTML (Hypertext Markup Language). This information is organized via hyperlinks, which bind the different pages into a tree structure. In addition to HTML, the Web can also use other file types, such as PDF (Portable Document Format), Flash (a multimedia platform), and image formats. Dynamic content can also be added via server-side scripting languages such as JavaScript or PHP, or via web services.

The domain name of every URI may contain anywhere from 3 to 63 characters, from the ISO basic Latin alphabet. Domain names are structured as follows:

    domain name    = root *( id tack root ).

The root may be empty, but it is frequently the first character of the domain name.

The IDTL (International Domain of the Liberty) root is allocated to the national domain level two (Naming Authority Pointer) identifier of ISO3166-1alpha-2.

The Temple of the Tooth (Dalada Maligawa) is a Buddhist temple in Kandy, Sri Lanka and is located in the royal palace complex of the former Kingdom of Kandy. Since ancient times, the Temple of the Tooth has been the places where the relic of the canine tooth of Buddha is kept. The relic was extracted from his cremation pyre in India in the 3rd century CE.
2. Kandy Lake
Kandy Lake, also known as the Body of Water of the Sacred Tooth, is an artificial lake in the center of the city of Kandy, in Sri Lanka.

The museum building consists of two sections, the Mediated Monarchial Hall and the Main Exhibition Hall. The Main Exhibition Hall contains sections for the history of Buddhism in Sri Lanka, the Kandyan kingdom, Dutch colonial period, art, and cultural artifacts.

Mining in Cornwall and Devon has been taking place since the Bronze Age. Cornish copper was highly regarded in the Roman period: the silver-alloy penny was first mined in the area around Tintagel in the seventh century, and subsequently came under the control of the Crown.

By the early fifteenth century, tin was being mined in Cornwall; the way was open for the Industrial Revolution and the rise of mass production, leading to an increased demand for metal. In 1852, just one year after the Great Exhibition, the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Society, founded in 1832, changed its name to the Royal Cornwall Institution for Science and Literature. It later became the Royal Institution of Cornwall.

In the late 19th century, extraction was mechanised, and many families in the mining areas relied upon copper for a living. Mining was a dangerous activity, and mining accidents were relatively common. The worst mining disaster in Britain occurred at StJust, on 26 October 1833, when 164 men and boys died when the Methuen copper mine flooded.

By the 1930s, the Cornish copper industry had ceased to be profitable and all mines were closed by 1950, with only the Parys Mountain Mine remaining in business

What Is the Foreshortening in Art?
When light travels to our eyes, it triggers our nerves, which is translated to the brain as an image. Since the density of these nerves is different in different parts of the eye, the image has a slight curved shape.
This curve is of 22.5 degrees in the eye. In a normal situation, the image we see is clear and the curve is not visible. But what if we, for some reason, see an image that has been drawn or sculpted in such a way that we are seeing it from an angle. In this case, since the curve of the original image is not visible, what we see is actually a reconstruction of the missing parts of the image. This reconstruction, however, presents a foreshortening.
The foreshortening is the difference between the actual shape of the object and the way it appears to the viewer. This difference can be due to the fact that the viewer is seeing the object from a different angle, or because the object is 3D and the viewer is only seeing it in 2D.
Foreshortening can be used to create different effects in art, such as making an object appear more dramatic, powerful, or realistic.

<human>: Can you please explain how the business cycle affects the economy?
<bot>: The business cycle is an up-down cycle in the economy's performance, typically measured by the gross domestic product (GDP), a broad measure of economic activity. The GDP represents the total dollar value of all of the goods and services sold in an economy in a specific time period.

The business cycle is a natural and necessary economic phenomenon that occurs over time in all economies. It reflects the flow of production and consumption in an economy. A cycle is composed of four phases: expansion, peak, recession, and recovery. During the expansion phase, the economy grows steadily, factories produce more goods, and consumers spend more money. The peak phase occurs when the economy reaches its highest point and then begins to slow down, often due to high inflation or interest rates, an overabundance of goods and services in the market, or other economic factors. The recession phase is characterized by a decline in economic activity, job losses, and low consumer confidence. The recovery phase is when the economy stabilizes and begins to grow again.

In addition, the 64-bit version of Windows has more than one data type, while the 32-bit version of Windows has only one: integer. The three types of integer data are signed, unsigned, and long. The signed type is also known as a "real" type, because it represents a fixed number of significant digits after the decimal point. Jomsom Muktinath Trek is an exciting trip that takes you through some of the most fascinating cultures, religions and history of Nepal. The trail passes through the beautiful Annapurna region, the Mustang district, and the Narayanhiti Royal Palace Museum in Kathmandu. You will get a chance to visit important cultural and religious sites, such as the Muktinath Temple, Gosaikunda Lake, and the Buddhist monasteries in Bai Temple. Along the way, you will pass through remote villages and meet the local people, who are friendly and welcoming. The trail is well-maintained and easy to follow, making it suitable for both experienced and beginner trekkers.

The best time to do the Jomsom Muktinath Trek is during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) seasons. During these seasons, the weather is mild and sunny, making it the perfect time to enjoy the beautiful views of the Himalayas. Spring is especially popular for bird-watchers, as the region is home to many species of birds that migrate from Siberia during this time of year. Autumn is also a good time to avoid the summer monsoon season and the associated mud and landslide

This was due to the major Roman campaigns against the Marcomanni, a Germanic people who lived on the northern bank of the Danube. In 9 CE, the area became part of the Roman Empire.

After the fall of the Western Roman Empire, the area was conquered by the Alemanni, and completed conquered by them in the 6th century. Once territories of the Roman Empire formally fell under the dominion of the Franks, they started to emerge as a powerful force in the late 5th and 6th centuries. As I have been discussing the magic of wordpress for building websites, I feel it is very important to understand a few basic terms.
WordPress is an acronym for PHP/MySQL, which is a server-side language used to create dynamic Web pages. WordPress is an open-source software, which means it is freely available to download, use, and modify without restriction from the WordPress website (https://wordpress.org/).
WordPress uses a model-view-controller ( MVC) architecture, which is a design pattern used to structure software applications.
The model layer handles data storage and functionality.
The view layer is responsible for displaying data to the user.
The controller layer is in charge of communication between the view layer and the model layer. It is also responsible for handling the user’s input and coordinating the flow of the site.

WordPress is built on top of PHP and MySQL, which are both open-source server-side language and database management system, respectively. WordPress comes with a predefined structure and theme, which can be easily installed on your web host's server.

text:
Building a successful software platform is done by building software that people want to use, then making that software defensible through patent protection, and lastly, building a strong brand."

In the 1990s, this approach was successful for Netscape, which relied heavily on its browser software, Netscape Navigator. Netscape's legal department supported the company's software developers by writing most of the legal language in the license agreements of this proprietary product. This approach made the software difficult for competitors to copy. One of the most well-known examples of this approach is the "Click-thru" license, which prohibited the copying of any part of the browser's interface, including buttons, bars, and images, as well as any use of Java applets and cookies.[24]

However, this approach was not without its flaws, as it was later found that the license agreements had the potential to infringe on the licenses of open-source code, which was subsequently removed by Netscape.[25] Despite this, the Navigator browser was relatively successful, becoming the dominant browser on the market until 1997, when Microsoft released its own web browser, Internet Explorer.[26]

text:
Yesterday [12 Nov], at the Shoreditch Metropolitan Police Office, I registered myself as a suffragette."

"What made you do that?" I asked.

"The British government launched a brutal crackdown on suffragette activism in 1913, arresting more than 300 women in London alone. These women were subjected to horrific treatment in prison, including force-feeding when they went on hunger strike.

"The Metropolitan Police Office was one of the many places where these women were rounded up and held. It was there that I decided that I had to do something, so I registered myself as a suffragette."

I was shocked to hear her story. It was clear that she had faced great personal risk to fight for what she believed in.

"How long were you a suffragette for?" I asked.

"I remained a suffragette for the rest of my life. I was arrested multiple times for protesting and sentenced to jail time. I even went on a hunger strike in Winson Green Prison in Birmingham in 1918."

Prison was not easy for any woman, but for a smaller woman like Emmeline it must have been particularly brutal. – Yet she refused to give up.

<bot>: The utility monster is a concept in game theory used to represent a household that is hard to impoverish. This is a hypothetical household that can afford to consume unlimited amounts of any good or service available in the economy, regardless of its impact on the supply and demand of that good or service.

The utility monster's existence is critical to understanding the US national debt. The US government borrows money to pay for programs and projects that it cannot fund through taxes and other revenue. This borrowed money is added to the national debt, which is currently over $31 trillion.

The debt limit, or the amount of money the US government is authorized to borrow, is a restriction imposed on the federal government's ability to issue debt. When the government approaches the debt limit, it may need to take extraordinary measures to continue borrowing money, such as suspending investments in government programs and selling off assets.

Well it's been a funny few days. On Friday, I travelled down to London to attend the Pride in London march. I went with my friend Simon, who happens to be gay, and we positioned ourselves at the front of the march, as we had permits for flags and megaphones. We were stationed next to journalist and presenter Gok Wan, which was quite a surreal experience, as you may have seen on TV. We were also positioned right in front of the Pride in London banner, which was being held up by a crew of volunteers. So most of the time, I was either next to or directly in front of Gok Wan. I kid you not.

After the march, we went to The Clarence Pub near St. James Park, where we were greeted by hundreds of people lining the streets, cheering and taking photos as the marchers went by. It was such an incredible moment, and a testament to the progress that has been made on LGBT rights in the past few years.

I received the brass that you sent me, and I have begun the electrolytic polishing. This work is very time consuming, and I have found that the best way to do it is in two or three sessions. The reason for this is that if you attempt to do it in one session, the high frequency current can cause burn marks on the plating which destroys the surface and makes it difficult to complete the polishing. Also, the electrolytic polishing is very easy to get caught between the anodes and cathode poles, so you have to be careful when handling the brass. I have completed the first session and will process the rest of the brass in two or three sessions after I finish the first. I will keep you informed.

As of yesterday, I have completed the first electrolytic polishing session. The result is shown in the photo. As you can see, there is still some copper showing on the surface, but it is hidden by the nickel plating. I will take the brass to the bathroom and work on it under a very bright light, as shown in the photo, as the next step in the electrolytic polishing process.

In 1850, the Congress of the United States over the veto of President Zachary Taylor adopted the Fugitive Slave Act, which provided for enforcement of the return of slaves across national lines. The Act made it US Federal Law to cooperate in the capture and return of runaway slaves. However, the law also made it a crime for an individual to help a runaway slave by, for example, providing food, shelter, or medical care. Anyone who harbored or helped a slave that had run away from a slave owner could be fined up to $1,000 and/or imprisoned for up to six months.
