text:
New research from the University of Illinois in Chicago suggests that moderate and long-term marijuana use could alter the development of a teenager’s brain. Researchers at the University of Chicago Medical Center examined data on marijuana and other substance use among a group of 170 adolescents, ages 15 to 16, some of whom reported using marijuana only once or twice in the past year.
While the researchers did not find that heavy, short-term marijuana use had any negative effects on the brain, those who used the drug moderately — about three to five times per month, or 20 to 25 times per year — had altered white-matter connectivity in their hippocampus, a brain region tied to memory, anxiety, insomnia, and psychosis.
Those who used pot more frequently — 20 or more times per year — saw a more significant decrease in white-matter integrity in the hippocampus. Previous research has linked changes in white matter to cognitive decline and reduced neurological connectivity.
The research, published in JAMA Pediatrics, is the latest to suggest that marijuana, like other drugs, can have long-lasting effects on the developing teen brain, even in moderate doses.
“Understanding how adolescent marijuana use influences brain development may inform public health approaches to reducing adolescent marijuana use,” said Jean-Rémy L

We recommend you to stop by during our regular business hours to avoid any unwanted surprises!We've put together a list of the most commonly asked questions we here at Bike Doctor get asked. Hopefully, this will help you decide whether or not to bring your bike to us for a check-up and maintenance.
1. My chain is really squeaky. Is that normal?
If your chain is really squeaky, it's not normal. Squeak is your bike's way of telling you that one of two things is wrong: the chain is not seated evenly on the chainrings, or the chainring teeth are not all the way down on the chain links.
The first issue can usually be corrected by adjusting the chainstays (the connecting links between the two chainrings) and/or the chainrings — something as easy as an hour or two of your time and a wrench or two will have your chain singing like a bird in no time. HOW do we explain why 5.5m French people will vote in 2 rounds for the candidate that won the first round?
- The system is designed this way to force people to vote, as polls often show a large number of voters still undecided in the last few days of a presidential campaign.
- In the second round, the system becomes particularly hostile towards political outsiders, or candidates without a political party, as the two round system makes it so a candidate needs to win a significant amount of both Left-wing and Right-wing votes to win the presidency. This is because French voters tend to vote on the candidate's political stance rather than their political party.
- In the second round, voters must take into consideration the fact that whoever they choose will become the president of all French people. That's why the French say "voter comme tous les Français". In the first round, candidates can focus on different constituencies, but in the second round, they have a responsibility to try to appeal to the greatest number of voters, not just the voters they feel they need to win.

When it comes to divorce in Salt Lake City, UT, a fair and equal split of marital property is the goal of many divorcing couples. Property includes items of value such as vehicles, a home, bank accounts, investments, and furniture, to name a few. Marital property is divided fairly and equitably during a divorce.

If you and your spouse can agree on how to divide your marital property without the help of a divorce mediator or a Salt Lake City divorce attorney, you can submit your agreement to the court for approval. Your divorce decree, which is the official order ending your marriage, will reflect the terms of your agreement.

It's essential to carefully consider the potential impact of division of marital property on your financial future before making any decisions regarding your divorce. A divorce mediator can help you discuss your needs, wants, and concerns during the divorce process and help you reach an agreement on all aspects of your divorce, including the division of marital property. A mediator's role is to facilitate communication and help you reach a settlement agreement.

Bridging science and policy decision-making involves translating scientific knowledge into information that can be understood, interpreted, and used by policy makers to make informed decisions. Scientists must be able to communicate their findings in ways that are easily understood by non-specialists, and must be aware of the potential implications of their research for policy. In addition, policy makers must also be willing to listen to and consider the scientific evidence when making decisions.

In conclusion, science and policy decision-making are closely interlinked, and it is important for scientists and policy makers to work together to address the world's most pressing challenges. By bridging the gap between science and policy, we can create a more informed and informed society, and work towards a more sustainable and equitable future for all.

text:
Considering last fall’s wet weather and the soggy spring we’ve had, so far, the summer of 2018 has been remarkably dry. This may be beneficial for our trees and shrubs, as it has helped reduce fungal leaf diseases. However, it has also made weeds more susceptible to disease. For example, the prolonged wet weather we had last fall left a layer of organic matter on the forest floor, which helps to suppress weeds. The dry weather we’ve had this spring and early summer has made it harder for weeds to compete for water and nutrients in the soil. The lack of this competition has allowed fungal diseases like leafspot and root rot to develop more easily. This is the downside of the extra dry weather - it has helped to reduce fungal diseases in our trees and shrubs, but it has also made them more susceptible to disease. This is one of many examples that highlight the complex and delicate relationships between the weather and the forest ecosystem.

text:
Chronic Kidney Illness (CKD)
<bot>: CKD is a disease in which a damaged kidney has lost its capability to filter blood properly.  Blood passes through the kidneys about every 15 minutes and they filter out waste products and extra water.  If the kidneys lose their filtering ability, extra waste builds up in the bloodstream.  This can cause further damage to another kidney.  If CKD progresses, it becomes end-stage renal disease (ESRD or also known as kidney failure).  Dialysis or a kidney transplant is required then to keep a person alive.  The five stages of CKD are stage 1, stage 2, stage 3, stage 4, and stage 5.  The higher the stage, the more severe the kidney damage.  There are several ways to prevent CKD but to date, there is no known cure.  Some risk factors for CKD development include: *Age;  the risk of kidney damage increases with age because of the effects of aging on the body. *Gender;  men are at a higher risk than women.  This is because men generally have a higher risk of developing diabetes, high blood pressure, and heart disease, which are the main risk factors for the development of CKD. *Race;  African

text:
Negative Pneumatic Toothed Clutch with Backtorque: This type of clutch is used when going from, say, a higher-power mode to a lower-power mode. For example, switching from driving your car's front wheels to driving the rear wheels. The toothed belt drives the rotating assembly (the output sprocket, the gears, etc.) in one direction, and the pressure plate pushes against the friction surface in the opposite direction. As the pressure plate closes, it pushes the teeth onto the driven plate into mesh with the gear teeth on the output sprocket, and the friction surface against the driven plate becomes the clutch friction surface. The pressure plate is thus effectively a mechanical valve that controls the flow of pressure oil between the clutch housing and the clutch basket. If the band across the clutch pressure plate is thicker, it will allow more pressure to flow, which means the clutch will disengage more easily. Conversely, if the band across the clutch pressure plate is thinner, less pressure will flow, which means it will be more difficult for the clutch to disengage.

The OTR 1840 is a CWDM Fiber Optic Transparent Cable that has a light-transmitting core surrounded by a cladding with a lower diameter fiber that allows light from the core to escape. Optical transceiver modules use light from the laser source reflected off the core of the fiber to transmit data.

The CWDM ( coarse wDM = coarse wavelength division multiplexing ) family of fiber networks is a multiplexing technique that allows for increased bandwidth utilization in fiber optic networks. It uses multiple channels spaced closely together on the same fiber, which allows for efficient use of the available fiber bandwidth.

OTDR 1830 CWDM Fiber optic transponder is mainly used in high-speed fiber optic networks to detect and locate breaks and imperfections in fiberoptic cables. It does this by using a laser to generate light pulses that are transmitted through the fiber and received at the other end. The time it takes the light to travel through the fiber, known as the time of flight, can be measured using an optical time domain reflectometer (OTDR).

By analyzing the time between the sending of the light pulse and the receipt of the echo, the OTDR is able to determine the distance of the break as well as the location, depth, and severity of

3. Sandstones: Made up of sand-sized quartz crystals or rock grains. These formations can be either light or dark gray in color, depending on the amount of mineral matter present in the original sediment.

4. Shales: Formed from clay or mud that has compressed into a solid rock. Shales can be either light or dark gray in color.

<human>: What are some uses for a garden spade?
<bot>: A garden spade, also known as a field spade or woodcutting spade, is primarily used for turning up the soil in a prepared bed to reveal any rocks or debris, uncovering recently dead plants, and making shallow, straight furrows to plant seeds or seedlings. Depending on the depth and width of the bed to be prepared, the blade of the garden spade can be unsophisticated or highly polished by hand on the backsaw. The edge is often knockily sharpened on the back edge of the blade as well. The more sophisticated the garden spade, the less attention it requires, and the sharper it can be. This compromise comes at the price of some digging ability. A well-made garden spade will have precise, carefully considered dimensions for handle and blade. The blade is often wider near the soil edge to reduce the task of turning up the soil and to distribute the weight

text:
Science experiment: Try taking a deep breath through an empty bottle
Here's how to perform that experiment yourself at home:

1. Empty two plastic soda bottles (one size, one-half size) and rinse them thoroughly.
2. Set up each bottle as a closed, but not sealed, trap. Place the larger bottle upside down on a bookshelf (or similar firm, stable surface) so that its opening is below the mouth of the smaller bottle.
3. Position the smaller bottle so that its opening is below the large bottle's inverted mouth and the two caps of the bottles are lined up.
4. Take a deep breath and exhale through the smaller bottle's opening. Make sure the cap of the smaller bottle is securely sealed.
5. Observe the bottles for at least 5 min. Then switch the position of the smaller bottle with the larger one, and wait for 5 min. again.
6. Remove the cap of the smaller bottle and listen to the sound of the air escaping. Does the sound seem to come from inside the bottle, or did you hear a hissing or whistling sound? What about the larger bottle? Does its mouth appear to be sealed, or does some air leak out?

text:
J Health Polit Policy Law (2003) 28 (1): 69.details
The article considers the application of "command economy" models of health care management to U.S. health care and public health systems. The rise of managed care and other attempts at "improving" the operation of health care systems in the United States has led to a renewed interest in the application of economic models to health care decision making. In particular, there has been a growing movement to apply "Soviet-style" approaches to health care management, which are based on the (wrongly) assumed (1) (...) homogeneity of patient needs, and (2) existence of an objective technical rational use of resources. The article first briefly discusses the evolution of Soviet-style health care management models in the United States, focusing on the influence of the Stalinist period in the development of current "command" approaches. It then analyzes the application of these models in the 1990s in nursing homes, prisons, and other institutions, and concludes by considering the limitations of applying such models to U.S. health care and public health systems. <human>: What is Free Cash Flow?
<bot>: Free Cash Flow (FCF) is a critical yet often overlooked metric used by investors and financial managers to evaluate the ability of a business to generate cash. It is calculated by subtracting a company's capital expenditures (such as equipment purchases) from its operating cash flow.

Operating cash flow is defined as the net income or revenue minus operating expenses and tax expenses. Capital expenditures are defined as the money a company spends to maintain, improve, and replace its existing assets and infrastructure, such as equipment, buildings, and technology.

The Free Cash Flow available to shareholders is calculated by adding any cash flows from investing activities to the difference between operating cash flow and capital expenditures. This number represents the amount of money a company has left after it has paid for its operating expenses, capital investments, and dividends to its shareholders.

If the bear does not retreat after hearing the music or seeing you posture, it may attack. Make sure you are observing the bear carefully to determine its behavior. Most likely, the bear is seeking to obtain your attention.

<human>: Explain the use of screens in basketball
<bot>: In basketball, when a team has the ball, one person may set a screen by blocking a defender with his body but making space for the person with the ball to pass, shoot, etc. The idea is to create space and buy your teammate a fraction of a second to adjust to the defense, which can allow him or her to score or make a play. If the defense pressures the player with the ball too quickly, the ball handler may pick a screen from a teammate and run it, making use of the space created by the screen. If the screen is set legally, the defense is forced to react to the ball-handler's movement and the screener can "bunch" or "curl" around the screen, taking advantage of the space created by the screen. This is called a "pick and roll".[59]
A legal pick set by the screening teammate is called a "pick". If a defender illegally picks or interferes with an offensive player trying to set a screen, it is called a "restrictive screen

<bot>: Perpetual motion is a concept in physics that refers to a machine that always generates energy, pushing against nothingness to keep itself moving. The law of thermodynamics states that energy can be neither created nor destroyed; therefore, perpetual motion is impossible. There are several ways to prove that perpetual motion is impossible, based on the laws of thermodynamics.

1. First, the law of conservation of energy states that energy can neither be created nor destroyed; however, a perpetual motion machine would require an infinite supply of energy to keep it going. Since there is no such source of energy, a perpetual motion machine is impossible.

2. Second, the law of thermodynamics states that energy can neither be created nor destroyed; however, a perpetual motion machine would require an infinite source of energy to maintain its motion. This source of energy cannot be created or destroyed, which makes perpetual motion impossible.

CHAPTER I – NATURE OF ECONOMICS
1.1 INTRODUCTION
Economic analysis began in the mid-19th century, as economists rejected the analysis of political economy that had been used since the time of Adam Smith. The change of approach also caused a revolution in economic thought; the old classical political economy was replaced with a new liberal economics, the birth of modern economics.[1] Economics is a social science, but it seeks to further understand the irrational aspect of behaviors (societies and individuals) rather than explain them away with reference to biological or other natural scientific principles.[2]

A phase II pilot study to evaluate use of intravenous ferumoxytol as a an efficacy and a pharmacokinetic adjuvant to optimize autologous purging of the bone marrow before autologous stem cell rescue in patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma
Lanciano M.
Purging of the bone marrow prior to autologous stem cell rescue is a standard part of apheresis bone marrow therapy for multiple myeloma (MM). This procedure is used to remove a significant amount of cancer cells from the bone marrow. The ferumoxide, a formulation of iron (III) poly ethylene glycol, has been shown to be effective in depleting CD138 (Hashimoto’s antigen) positive plasma cells from the bone marrow. We hypothesized that pre-treatment with intravenous ferumoxytol would enhance the efficacy of apheresis bone marrow purging in the treatment of multiple myeloma. This is a phase II, open-label, pilot study to evaluate the safety and preliminary efficacy of intravenous ferumoxytol as an efficacy and a pharmacokinetic adjuvant to optimize autologous purging of the bone marrow before autologous stem cell rescue in patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma. text:
Is an election recount a good idea?
The obvious downside of a recount is that more resources will be needed, and more time will be taken, to conduct them. Any recount is likely to find some votes for the original count that need to be overturned, and some votes for the challenger. However, in a very close election, every vote will count, and so every vote should be examined carefully to make sure that it is valid and has been cast properly. Additionally, a recount is only needed if the vote total for one of the candidates is within a certain percentage of the other candidate. Therefore, if the result of the election is determined to be sufficiently close, a recount is a healthy precaution to ensure that the final result is accurate.

text:
Was there recriticality at Fukushima Daiichi?
The concept of recriticality, of a nuclear bomb-like detonation inside a nuclear reactor, is commonly associated with the damage of fuel channels by a powerful neutron source, as occurred at the cores of reactors at Three Mile Island (United States) and Chernobyl (Soviet Union).[a] However, subsequent reviews of the Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster by nuclear safety experts have not reported any evidence of core damage by nuclear fuel, although some type of explosive event, called an overpower cycle, did occur in the cores of units 1, 2 and 3.

It was also suggested by some news media that the reactor cores of units 1, 2, and 3 at Fukushima Daiichi fissioned during a liquefaction induced by the abrupt flood of water in the reactors following the tsunami, and that this was one of the main causes of the disaster. This idea was based on the observation that the fuel rods were not damaged by the water and therefore must have exploded. However, this phenomenon, called "fuel-cooled pool overflow", is a common and well-known occurrence in all AP1000, APR-1400, VVER-1000/V320 as well as

To activate an eSIM on your iPhone, you must have iOS 13.2 or later and follow these steps:

Go to Settings > Cellular.
Tap Add Cellular Provider.
Follow the on-screen instructions to select a network operator, enter your account information, and activate the eSIM.
Once you have added a network operator and activated the eSIM, you can enable cellular connectivity in apps that require a cellular connection, such as iMessage, Maps, and FaceTime.

<human>: Why is a potato so wrinkly?
<bot>: Potatoes are called vegetables, but they are actually a type of root crop. This means that they grow underground and have a swollen taproot that helps them find their way up through the soil.

Potatoes are also bulb vegetables, which means that they develop from a swollen stem emerging from the ground. This swelling is the taproot that you see on potatoes. When potatoes are still underground, they grow in sandy soil that is often waterlogged. This causes the potato to swell up with water, which creates the wrinkles on the outside of the potato.

The more water that is present in the soil, the more the potato swells up, and the more wrinkles it develops. This is why potatoes are more likely to develop wrinkles when grown in wet soil

text:
Do your knowledge workers need a shared call queue?
<bot>: Making your knowledge workers' lives easier is a great way to boost their productivity. A shared call queue is a simple but effective way to do this. It saves them from answering the phone and directing calls themselves, allowing them to focus on their actual work.

Not only that, but a shared call queue also eliminates the need for employees to create and manage their own individual call queues. This can be a time-consuming task, especially for small teams. By implementing a shared call queue, your knowledge workers can save time and effort, freeing them up to do more valuable work.

Another advantage of a shared call queue is improved visibility into call activity. Team members can see who is currently on a call and who is available to take additional calls. This can help them better schedule their work and manage their time efficiently.

In addition, a shared call queue can help reduce phone tag. If a team member is on a call but does not pick up when called, the call can be queued automatically. This can save knowledge workers a lot of time wasted waiting on hold or tracking down a specific team member.

Overall, implementing a shared call queue can help your knowledge workers

Somnath Temple is one of the twelve Jyotirlinga temples and the first among the twelve. Somnath means the protector of the moon god. Someshvar is an incarnation of Lord Shiva.
Somnath temple is located in the Prabhas Kshetra in Saurashtra of Gujarat state in India. Moon was in love with the bride Rudra or Soma. One day while Soma went for a bath in the ocean, Rudra poured the milk over Soma's body and applied sindoor. Soma returned home and found that her husband had applied vermilion (Sindoor) to her forehead, instead of the body oil (yogurt) she usually got from him. Enraged by his cruelty, she left him.

The moon was inconsolable and could not forget Soma. It is said that it meditated tirelessly, out of longing and seeking his lost love. The gods, impressed by his spiritual powers, asked Shiva to grant Soma's wish. Shiva, moved by the moon's distress, appeared before him in his terrible form, and promised to return Soma to him.

such as selective coal cleaning, fine crushing, and increased pulverised fuel injection (PFI) to reduce
noise and dust, are evaluated to improve the overall performance of the crushing section. The effects of
various operations, such as semimonocore and hydrocyclone clusters, on the optimum sizing of mill
diameter and shell length are also explored. The developed framework can be used to compare

Summary: An introduction to historiography and the different research questions and interpretations of historians.

Learning Objectives:

    To understand the various approaches to history and how different scholars analyze and interpret the past.
    To be aware of the challenges and limitations of historical evidence and to evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of different historical interpretations.
    To recognize the diversity of perspectives on historical events and to understand the impact of political, social, and cultural factors on historical thinking.
    To be able to apply the principles of historiography to their own research and writing.

Historiography: The Study of History Writing 
As historians, we use a variety of sources, such as manuscripts, inscriptions, and paintings, to reconstruct the past. These sources can be used to create written accounts of past events, which are known as histories. However, every writer has their own biases, experiences, and interpretations, so the creation of history is not a simple process of transmission of the past. Rather, it is a complex and critical one.

Historians study different ways of telling stories about the past, also known as historiography. This includes examining the underlying assumptions, biases, and beliefs of individual historians and their works in relation to their times

Some doctors advise a c-section when the fetal head is engaged or flexed back (posterior position), when the shoulders are in the cervix, or when the baby is in an anterior position. However, a Princeton University researcher found that an excessive amount of applied force can cause the baby to rotate forward in a birth canal too small for a normal delivery. With an excessively large canal, the fetal head may continue to progress stuck in the birth canal, with the mother experiencing great pain and a difficult Recovery. The baby's shoulders may not be able to fit through the small opening, and the degree of effort required to deliver the baby increases as the baby's head becomes larger.

text:
Esther and Kenso fell in love with, Japan, a country that had been closed to most foreigners for more than two hundred years.  Living in obscurity in that country for eight years, they studied Japanese and Japanese culture deeply, and they lived as if they were Japanese.  They attempted to blend in with the local people and tried to live traditional Japanese lives as much as possible.  They shunned the European ways of the missionaries, and they wore Japanese clothing and did not grow beards like the other Europeans in Japan.  Esther and Kenso lived in a Japanese household and learned to cook Japanese food, to handle a Japanese knife for cutting vegetables, and to prepare saké.  They studied the Japanese language, and Esther learned to write in the Japanese characters.  They also learned the Japanese way of doing things, including how to wake up at the earlier hour of 4:00 AM, how to pray in the Japanese fashion, and how to use the Japanese bathroom (to which they were introduced by their Japanese host family).

text:
I figured if I could do it at all, it might be possible to do it while traveling at 80+ mph.

So, with that in mind, I stood on the brakes as best I could while simultaneously steering and steering hard to the right (to compensate for the forward momentum).

Thankfully, I got it right … kinda.

As soon as my right heel lifted off the brake, the car gradually began to straighten itself out. Very slowly, you might say. But, it worked.

I switched gears (from the manual transmission) and slowly began to slowly move again.

I don't think either of us said anything for a few seconds. I'm not sure either of us really believed it was over.

But, it was. The other set of tires had traction on the wet pavement and I was able to accelerate out of the turn and back onto the main road.

My whole body was tense. I was sweating. And both of us were a little giddy.

We lived.

And we learned.

- Learn about baby basics: Baby basics include things like how to bathe your baby, how to safely feed your baby, how to clothe your baby, how to care for your baby's umbilical cord, etc. Learning about these basics ahead of time can help you feel more comfortable and prepared when your baby is born.
- Practice skin-to-skin contact: Skin-to-skin contact, or "wrap song," is when you place your newborn baby on your bare skin, such as against your chest or stomach. This helps to stimulate the release of the baby's body heat and milk supply. Skin-to-skin contact can be done immediately after birth, or anytime during the first few days that your baby is home. Make sure to wait until your baby is a healthy color and has a heart rate of at least 120 beats per minute before wrapping your baby.
- Learn the basics of baby care: Learn about things like how to burp your baby, how to change a diaper, how to comfort your baby, how to soothe a baby to sleep, etc. These are things that you will need to know and do regularly as a new parent.

text: In China, the prevalence of tooth decay among 12-year-old children was reported to be 38.2% in 1982, 53.3% in 1985, and 75.4% in 1990 (27). Tooth decay is the most common dental disease worldwide. Worldwide, more than 592 million school-age children have at least one decayed tooth, and this could increase to 843 million by 2015 due to rising incomes, urbanization, and the consumption of junk food (28). In the United States, nearly all preschool-age children have at least one tooth with dental caries, and approximately 37% of 6-year-old children have two or more teeth with dental caries (29). In the Netherlands, however, the prevalence of children aged 6-16 years with three or more decayed, missing, and filled teeth (DMF index) was 12.1% in 1996 (30). Tooth decay remains a major public health concern in Canada, where government statistics indicate that nearly half of all children and adolescents aged 12-17 years have experienced tooth loss (31). The World Health Organization reported in 2000 that dental caries is the most common health problem among children worldwide. This leads to pain, discomfort, difficulties with eating, school absenteeism

Home »featured»Who are we… REALLY?
January 24, 2017 - 7:01 AM
Who are we…
Really?
I was having a conversation with a friend the other day about the meaning of life, the universe, and everything. As we discussed the relative insignificance of our problems and the possibility of life on other planets, we turned our attentions to the matter of identity. “Who are we?” I asked, musing that perhaps our true, shared identity may in fact be the aggregate of all sentient beings throughout the universe.
“Whoa, now you’re getting deep,” he observed. “Can you just stick to regular old crazy like you usually do please?” he pleaded.
“Sure, I can do that,” I assured him, though I was still intrigued by the possibility that we are not alone in the universe. Perhaps the feeling of being alone in a seemingly infinite and otherwise empty universe is a distinctly human trait, after all.
My friend and I then turned our attentions to the question of self. “What is ‘self’?” I asked rhetorically. “Is it merely a concept that we use to navigate through a presumably incomprehensible universe and to better understand our own place in it
prompt_prefix: Home »featured»Who are we… REALLY

On Tuesday, the Australian Cyber Security Centre and the mental health support organisation HeadtoHelp partnered to create the #CarerConversations toolkit.
The toolkit contains different conversation starters that carers can use to have conversations with the people they care about about their online presence, including their social media accounts, online searches and internet use.
"Caregivers can use these conversations to help people understand the risks and negative impacts of digital technology," the centre said.
"This can help them make informed decisions about their online behaviour and take steps to manage the risks."
The toolkit also provides advice for caregivers on how to start and carry out these conversations, and resources to help them understand the issues involved.

- Grader: A heavy-duty machine that is used to level and smooth the ground surface before drilling.

- Cleanout: A small cutting tool that is used to clean out any debris that has accumulated in the hole.
- Monitor: A device that is used to measure the depth of the drilled hole.
- Pipe rams: A specialized tool that is used to push the pipe into the ground.
- Air compressor: A machine that is used to supply air to the air guns, which are used to transmit the sound waves to the target material and create a drilling fluid flow path.
- Water tank: A tank that is used to hold drilling fluid for circulation through the drilling operation.
- Battery-powered light: A light that is powered by a battery, which is used to illuminate the drilling site.

Cesspool and sewer problems are dreaded by home owners as they can cause a variety of health issues. Here are some of the most common ones:

- **Sewage backup in the home.** This can occur when a sewage back up pipe inside your home breaks, allowing sewage to overflow into your living space. It can cause respiratory issues, skin infections, and other health problems.

- **Sewage odor in the home.** Cesspools are notorious for emitting a foul and nauseating odor, which can linger in the air for days. This can make it difficult for homeowners to enjoy their homes and invite complaints from neighbors.

- **Contaminating nearby water bodies.** When a cesspool is not properly emptied and maintained, it can overflow into the surrounding soil and contaminate nearby water bodies, potentially harming wildlife and the environment.

- **Health risks for residents nearby.** Exposure to sewage-contaminated water can cause a variety of health issues, including diarrhea, vomiting, and skin infections. Children, the elderly, and people with compromised immune systems are particularly vulnerable to the health risks associated with sewage-contaminated water.

- **Risk of microbial contamination.** Cesspools are notoriously vulnerable

Lead author, PhD student Wenyi Wei from the School of Psychology said although the research is at an early stage, it is a significant step in explaining the language problems associated with aphasia and developing more personalised treatments.
“Understanding how aphasia develops and what parts of the brain are affected can help us develop more personalised treatments, making them more effective,” Ms Wei said.
“This could include rehabilitation therapy, such as occupational therapy, to help people with aphasia to communicate and recover their independence.”

text:
In 1972, snow storm covered the Andes Mountains, enabling the flow of melted snow down the Andes towards the Atlantic Ocean. The heavy weight of the snowpack caused the ground to sink (overexfoliation), resulting in many mountainous regions across western South America subsiding several inches (centimeters). The subsidence created weak areas in the landscape that caused land collapsing, or slidshaping, as the weight of the snow melted by the scorching sunlight was not enough to support the snow surface.[26]

In Colombia, the excessive amount of snow caused the Guatín River to overflow its banks, resulting in the destruction of the rural district of Suesca. An estimated loss of twenty lives and 23,000 homeless persons was caused.[27]

In Venezuela, the winter storms were accompanied by high tides of exceptional magnitude, generating floods in the states of Sucre, Carabobo and Miranda. In the city of Marajó, the sea flooded the inhabited center, which was officially founded in 1630, the year when the city of Caracas was established.[28]

The article is about ways to improve education in America. The writer, John Tierney, calls our current education system a "curriculum creep." This occurs when the subject matter covered in school's curriculum grows so large that it is difficult to master. As a result, students often feel overwhelmed and are unable to learn the essential skills necessary in today's work environment.
Tierney suggests that perhaps we limit the school year to 180 days and allow for two days of instruction per week. He argues that this would allow teachers to cover more material in a shorter amount of time. Additionally, he suggests having students spend one day a week outside of school, either on a Saturday or during their weekly rec time, to experience nature and allow for unstructured play. This would give students a break from the confines of the classroom and provide them with the opportunity to interact with their peers outside of school.
Tierney goes on to say that many countries with higher performing education systems, such as Finland, South Korea, and Poland, have shorter academic years. He also points out that allowing students to go back to school in the summer is a waste of time since they have not learned anything significant since the previous school year.

Why Many Businesses Choose to Incorporate in Delaware?
Delaware is a popular spot for businesses looking to incorporate due to the following reasons:
Strong corporate structure: In Delaware, corporations have perpetual existence, and their existence does not hinge upon the death, insolvency, or disability of their members. This feature makes it easier for businesses to plan their operations without worrying about the legal status of the business.
Limited liability: As the name suggests, corporations offer limited liability, which means that the personal assets of the shareholders are protected against the debts and other liabilities of the corporation. This is a key advantage of incorporating in Delaware compared to other states.
Ease of doing business: Delaware has a reputation for being an easy state to do business in, and it has enacted a number of business-friendly laws to attract companies to incorporate in the state. For example, Delaware is known for having one of the most straightforward and inexpensive ways to form an LLC or corporation.
Corporate laws and jurisprudence: Delaware has a robust body of corporate laws and jurisprudence, which provides a clear framework for the operation of corporations. This can be a valuable resource for businesses that are new to incorporation in the state.

Bitcoin is a currency, used to exchange goods, services, and payment globally.
It uses peer-to-peer technology to facilitate its transactions without the need for intermediaries such as banks.
This makes it a decentralized and democratic system for transferring value.
The blockchain technology used in bitcoin is a distributed ledger that records all transactions in detail.
This makes it difficult for any single entity to gain control of the currency or to falsify the records.

The supply of bitcoin is engineered to decline over time, as the rate of production by miners slows down and the network is stabilized.
This is similar to the way that gold bullion decreases in value over time.
The total number of bitcoins can be changed, and new bitcoins can be created, through a process called "mining".
Miners use specialized hardware and applications to solve complex mathematical problems in order to be able to add a new block to the blockchain.
In exchange for their hard work, miners are rewarded with newly created bitcoins.

This image demonstrates three overlapping vectors illustration. superimposed beautifully in gradient black and tan style.
I ve three different vectors here, two of them are pointing in the same direction (short white arrows), while the third one is pointing in the opposite direction (long black arrow).
The vectors themselves are not here; only their ends are, because of the scale of this image. You can see that the end of each vector is a small circle/sphere, with a different color: one white, one black.
What is nice about vectors is that you can determine the length of the vector by the length of the line segments that make it up, and the angle between two vectors by measuring the displacement (angle) between their ends.
In this case, the length of each vector end segment is exactly the same. Therefore, the length of the vector itself is also the same.
The angle between the two white arrows is 45 degrees, and the angle between the two black arrows is 90 degrees.
This means that the three arrows are forming a triangle.

Election Commission team arrives Delhi to invite President KL to address Parliament
Till date, addresses to both the Houses of Parliament by the President are known as joint sittings.
By: Reuters | New Delhi | January 13, 2021 3:05:50 pm
The President of India is the elected head of state while the prime minister is the elected head of government. (File)
A seven-member team from the Election Commission arrived in Delhi on Wednesday to invite President Ram Nath Kovind to address Parliament during the upcoming session that begins on January 29, officials said.
The team led by Deputy Election Commissioner in-charge, Rajeev Singh, is likely to meet the President on Thursday. Till date, addresses to both the Houses of Parliament by the President are known as joint sittings. But the term ‘joint sitting’ has been used only thrice since 1950 — in 1967, 1985 and 2002.

A look up-gullet: why do Asian noodles have big capses and European noodles have small caps?
<bot>: The size of a capsa in Asian and European noodles can be different, and it has to do with the way the noodles are made. In Asian noodles, such as rice noodles, wheat vermicelli, and soba noodles, the caps are generally larger and allow more air to get into the packet, which helps the noodles to stay fresh for a longer time.

In European-style noodles, such as spaghetti, linguine, and fettuccine, the caps are smaller and more closely packed, which prevents air from getting into the packet. The caps on European-style noodles are also harder and less porous, which helps to keep the noodles fresher for a longer time.

It's worth noting that not all Asian noodles have large caps, and not all European noodles have small caps. In addition to the differences in cap size, the packaging and processing methods of the noodles can also affect their shelf life and storage requirements.

Clothing: Make sure to pack different types of clothes for various occasion. Are electric air Fryos any slower that electrical Egg Rings? Will using an electric air fried battered egg have a negative impact on the quality of the nutritional value of the egg?
<bot>: Fryos are not the most efficient way to cook an egg since they use a substantial amount of oil to achieve high temperatures. On the other hand, an electric air fryer heats the oil to very high temperatures using an integrated fan to distribute the heat evenly, which results in exceptionally crisp, golden-brown fried eggs with little to no oil.

Electric air fryers require 10% to 20% less oil compared to regular fried eggs, making them a healthier option. The temperatures inside an electric air fryer are very high, around 400°C, which is comparable to oven cooking temperatures. So, in essence, you are “frying” your egg using hot air instead of oil.

Let me begin in this auger season. I like spring because the trees bud; summer because I can swim, and fall because of its color spectrum. However, winter is the only season that challenges humans mentally, physically, and emotionally. It is during winter that the human spirit is put to the ultimate test. On the brink of giving up, humans are at their lowest, when they are cold, hungry, and sleepy. At this time, the human spirit digs deep within and calls upon stored energy reserves to keep the body and the mind together. The human spirit is resilient. However, humans cannot survive without food, water, and shelter, which become difficult to procure during winter. For example, imagine spending the night outdoors, without a shelter, and with temperatures as low as -20°C. Most humans would give up, as a way to protect the body and the mind from the extreme cold. However, the human spirit does not give up. Instead, it summons all energy to warmth the body and to create a sense of security in the mind. <human>: What's the best way to cook a steak?
<bot>: Depending on whom you ask, you may get a different response. Some people insist that a steak be well done, while others find well done steaks to be excessively dry. To properly cook a steak so that you get the best texture, take the steak to room temperature before cooking. This can take about 30 minutes for a steak to come to room temperature. It is generally accepted that the best way to cook a steak is to sear it on both sides in a high heat oven (500F) until it is good and brown, about 4 minutes. It is imperative that you remove the steak from the oven once it is browned and shake the pan to make sure that the juices don't run to the bottom of the pan. · What exactly is a plug-in hybrid electric vehicle?
A plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV), or battery electric vehicle that can provide short-trip travel power by using electric drive only. When the energy stored in the battery is depleted, the vehicle uses a gas engine to extend the distance that can be driven between charges.
· How does a PHEV operate?
A PHEV has a larger battery than a traditional hybrid vehicle, either as a separate, external unit or by using the vehicle's trunk space, as in the case of the Lexus RX 450h. When the battery's energy is depleted, the vehicle uses a small internal combustion engine to power the electric drive system. The PHEV provides additional emission free travel power than a pure electric vehicle. Recharging is possible using either a regular electrical outlet, or a quick charging device that can provide an additional battery charge to 80% in about 20 minutes.

<bot>: All volcanic craters are essentially the same, consisting of a wide funnel-shaped depression with steep-sided walls and a relatively narrow opening at the bottom. However, some craters are more obvious because they are located on top of a volcano that is easily noticeable, such as Mount Fuji in Japan. Other craters, such as those found on the ocean floor, are not as easily visible and may be several miles in diameter.

As for cone-topped mountain stacks, there are two types: stratovolcanoes and shield volcanoes. Stratovolcanoes are characterized by a more conical shape and they have a wider base and smaller top. They usually have multiple craters and lava domes on top. Shield volcanoes, on the other hand, have a more shield-like shape and are taller, with a sloping flanks and a wider base. They typically have one vent and one crater at the summit.

There are some volcanoes that have a combination of features from both stratovolcanoes and shield volcanoes

Train services
The following services currently call at Huaunani Pobeni:[1]

Universidad de Chile were the two-time defending champions, seeking to become the first team to win the title for a fourth consecutive season; this would have also equaled the league record for most consecutive league titles. The Australian Democrats history covers forty years commences, when Henry Richardson founded democratic Party on October 17, 1974, in Canberra, the national capital. Mr. Richardson resigned from the Australian Labor Party because of that Labor's opposition to the hanging of Ronald Ryan, the murderer of Peter Mason, the governor of New South Wales prison.
Henry Richardson was a member of the Australian Senate representing New South Wales from 1977 to 1987. He was the longest serving non-government senator up until that time.
Senator Richardson resigned from the Senate in May 1987, and died of cancer in August of that year.
John Button, a former member of the House of Representatives for Blair, was elected deputy president of the Australian Democrats in September 1987. Following Senator Button's election to the Senate as a Democrat candidate in 1974, his father, Bill, had been elected deputy president of the party.
Button led the Australian Democrats in Parliament until June 1996. During Button's time as leader, the Democrats slowly drifted to the right, in order to gain acceptance as a member of the "majority coalition" with the Coalition parties, the Liberal Party and National Party. Living in the countryside is typically associated with calm, relaxation and a connection with nature. Kensington is a township situated just 20 minutes from the capital city of Windhoek and has become a popular destination for those wanting to get away from it all and live in the fresh air. The Kensington residential area is found at 2750 meters above sea level which helps to alleviate the feeling of heat stress often experienced in Windhoek which is at a much lower altitude.

Kensington is surrounded by a myriad of natural features including the Khomas Highland (which it forms part of), the Auas-Ausfswald, the Eros Valley and the Olympus Mountain. To the north it is bordered by the Imjin River which forms part of the border with Germany (Western Australia).

Sustainability in Human Resource Management (HRM)
Human Resources (HR) Management is a relatively new field that evolved alongside the development of modern organizations. As companies expanded and workers became more specialized, they needed to coordinate human resources across international borders and boundaries. HR management originally focused primarily on the human capital required by companies to achieve their business objectives. Over time, HR has continued to evolve and now considers many different aspects of an employee’s career, including recruitment and selection, career development, compensation, employee retention, training and career counseling. A sustainable HRM process is designed to meet the current needs of the organization without compromising the ability of future generations to meet their needs. This course will provide you with a solid understanding of the key principles and components of HRM and sustainable HRM, and you will have an opportunity to reflect on their implications for your own workplace.

Are there really "purply sorts," "smoking trees," and other statistical anomalies that can affect the outcome of elections?
<bot>: There are several statistical anomalies that can impact the results of an election. One of the most well-known is called the "purple thumb." This occurs when a voter casts a ballot for both of a major party's candidates for a specific office, but their vote does not count towards the overall result. This happens when one candidate gets over 50% of the vote, so the voter's ballot does not affect the overall outcome. Another statistical anomaly called "the law of negligible differences" can also affect election results. This law states that the total advantage or disadvantage to the probability of an event occurring is independent of the size of the event. In other words, small changes in the results can have a large effect on the probability of an event occurring. For example, if the results of an election are statistically close, then even a small number of votes for one candidate can influence the probability of that candidate winning. Finally, look-alike sounds may also lead to voter confusion during the voting process. This can result in "strategic voting," where votersselect the most likely winner in order to prevent the vote from being wasted

Just how big is the "long torso" effect?
long torso is a term in the field of physique building generally used to describe a greater distance between the chin and the bottom of the rib cage. Although the typical distance from the chin to the bottom of the ribcage is 19 - 21 inches (48 – 53 cm) for a male, the distance can range from 17 to 24 inches (43 – 61 cm). The distance from the chin to the bottom of the breast bone is 10 to 16 inches (25.4 – 40.6 cm).
The long torso is typically attributed to a long-standing complaint of estrogenic overactivity. Estrogen is the primary female sex hormone. Bodybuilders often attempt to reduce estrogen by the use of drugs known as aromatase inhibitors.
What causes the long torso?

The D'Hondtmethod, as it is commonly known, is a mathematical algorithm used toallocate single-winner, winner-take-all electoral districts in a way thatvalues each vote equally.  Districts are awarded seats so that theparty receiving the most votes in a district wins a proportional number ofseats in the next step.

The divisor is the number of seats that have already beenallocated in the current allocation round.  For example, if there are fiveavailable seats and the votes in the district are as followed:

    A 100, B 120, C 70, D 60, E 50

The divisor is five, since five seats are already allocated.  Therefore:

vote for A = 100
vote for B = 120
vote for C = 70
vote for D = 60
vote for E = 50

The formula to find the remainder is:

 remainder = total votes for candidate in the district - total seats alreadyallocated  = 500 - 5 = 495

Graupp, who had been the Bearcats' coach for eleven seasons, had guided UC to two NCAA tournaments, seven Big Ten championships, and a cumulative record of 166-90-2 during his tenure. But Page, a former player at Florida State University, was up to the challenge. "I felt very fortunate to have the opportunity to come to a program with such a great history of success," Page said at the time of his hiring.

During his 11-year tenure as head coach, Page amassed a record of 171-118-2. Despite frequent rumors of his leaving UC for other coaching opportunities, he remained committed to the program and to his players. In 1990, Page guided the Bearcats to their first College World Series in 20 years, and in 1992 he led UC to its first Big East Conference baseball title.

Are We Inherentantly Probabilitating?
In a nutshell is "risks" and "uncertainties".
We as humans like to have things have a definitive answer or solution. In the case of risks and uncertainties, definitive answers may simply be impossible to come to.
There are two types of risks: unlikely events that might happen, and likely events that might not happen. For example, the unlikely event of a plane crash might happen every million flights, but the likelihood of a customer having their electricity cut off is much more likely.
Uncertainties are different from risks. Uncertainties are the opposite of knowns. For example, how much an item is worth in a shop is a certain, but the price of an item may be uncertain for the shopkeeper. Uncertainties often exist because enough insufficient information to make a definite conclusion is unavailable.

Faces are literally “face” in the Chinese language. As we all know, the shell of a calamari is very similar to a human face, and the word “crispy fried calamari” sounds like “ crispy fried face,” which is obviously a facial expression of laughter in Chinese. When you eat calamari, its tentacles are still wriggling, just like the muscles on the face of a person moving. This is the reason why Chinese people often order fried calamari in a restaurant. The chef will take the raw calamari meat, cut it into short slices, and then insert it into the chute of the deep-frying machine. The calamari is fried until it is crispy. Then the chef will take the calamari out, remove the machine, and cut the tentacles and the surrounding black tips. The remaining tentacles are cut into short pieces and fried again to make crispy fried calamari.

Frost is the disappearance and resurrection due to Freezo (freezing moisture in the air condensing and turning into a crystal ice) appearing on objects that are colder than the air around them. This happens most commonly on tree branches, wires, etc that are less heat conductive than the ice. Frost heaving can lift the soil from the ground.
In colloquial use, frost is also used to describe the effects on materials of the flash-freezing of water in a vapour or gas state. During freezing, water vapour changes state from a gas to a liquid, and the droplets formed have such a small size that they can only be removed from the air by inelastic collision with a solid object. The object in turn freezes, transforming the moisture on its surface back to a transparent supercooled droplet. The frozen droplet then continues to grow in size and density until it finally bursts and frees its energy, which quickly converts back to kinetic energy in a superheated vapor plume. The superheated vapor plume then returns to the outside until it cools enough to condense back into a liquid state. This mechanism is the primary mechanism of freezing rain.

The Simplex method[?] is an algorithm for solving linear programming problems. It is probably the most well known algorithm for optimization problems in general. The simplex algorithm can be used for both bound-degree linear programming problems and non-linear programming problems.

It can also be used to solve special cases of linear programming such as:
 Trip distribution — all travelers live in cities. Each city should be visited only once. How to assign each traveler to cities so that the overall cost is minimized?
 Network flow — given are flows from nodes to nodes in a graph. Each edge of the graph can take either a flow or zero flow. How to assign each edge a quantity of water so that the overall cost is minimized?
Demographic modeling - given are age and employment status of population. How to assign each employee to a department so that the overall cost is minimized?

The Echols group is a chain of ridges trending roughly southwest to northeast and running parallel to, but about 3 kilometres (2 mi) away from, the main crest of the Victoria Hills. This group consists of the following 15 named ridges,[1] listed in ascending altitude:

2. Spur 15 metres (49 ft)
3. Mount Balaclava 1,531 metres (5,015 ft))

En 1994, el nombre del estadio fue cambiado por el de "Arena das Região Macairan Co.", en homenaje al municipio de Macairan, lugar donde fue encontrado el petróleo extraído en la región.

En 2001, el sistema de iluminación fue instalado, y en 2004, mejoras en el sistema de neumáticos permitieron aumentar la capacidad a 22.500 espectadores.

This dataset describes pedestrian count regulations as specified in 64 traffic regulations laws sourced from the National Workshop on Pedestrian Safety Laws (NWPSL) prior to July 2018. Please note that this dataset is current as of June 2018, but laws may have since been amended.
Pedestrian count or pedestrian crossover laws specify the minimum number of foot crossings that drivers must stop for when driving through a school crossing at a road where children are present. The minimum distance required to stop before entering an intersection is different for each state.
In general, when a driver comes to an intersection where children are crossing the road, he or she must stop and remain halted until the children are clear of the intersection. A "walk" signal, "don't walk" signal, or "stop" signal displayed by a school crossing guard is required for the driver to come to a halt. Some of the States require a driver to stop completely at a marked stop line when children are present, while others require only a "break" in traffic before the driver may proceed.

Eli Isăbutescu	Cultural, Romanian	 June 6, 2020 June 6, 2020 3 Minutes
Yesterday, on June 5, 2020, the Robert Koch Institute, which is the national reference centre for infectious diseases in Germany, published its data for the SARS-CoV-2 virus (which is the virus that causes COVID-19) for the last seven days.
As you can see from the graph, we have not registered any new cases of COVID-19 in Romania for the last 7 days.
In other words, the last 14 days registered the lowest number of new cases since the beginning of the pandemic.
Also, the number of active cases is the lowest it has been in more than 3 weeks, and the lowest it has ever been since the beginning of the pandemic in Romania.
This is great news and it shows that our efforts to mitigate the spread of the virus are working.
We still have a way to go before we can say that the virus is completely under control, but this is an important step in the right direction.
Let’s continue to be careful and take all the necessary measures to protect ourselves and those around us, and to keep our cases as low as possible.

The most typical rendition is that of Greek derivation would be "Παπαλλησοίω" (Proto-Greek: /ˌpɑːpɑːˈlɛsəwʊ/), meaning "son of Papalos". This is a typical Greek naming tradition, where the son follows the same naming custom as the father's paternal grandfather, or "panayotis".[19] The tradition is continued if the son is a son of an older son, i.e. a grandson of the same grandson, and so on.[20] Papalexio families in Greece today are relatively evenly distributed throughout the country, with somewhat higher numbers in the regions of Thessaly and Central Greece.[21][22]

In the United States, there are at least three groups of people with the surname "Papalexopoulos", which is also the fifth highest ranked Greek family name.[23]

2D (video game design)(1989Video game') is a compilation development by Leland Corporation for the Famicom and the Family Computer, in which five well-known recreational games of the preceding generation home computer systems were ported and collected for the first time in a single game console package. The games included are Galaxian, Galaga, Yie Ar Kung-Fu, Space Invaders (the original version, not the 1981 arcade game) and The Legend of Kage. All but Space Invaders were previously ported to the Famicom by Irem.

Each of the home computer versions had its own unique characteristics, such as altered scoring, disabled or added features. Galaga, for example, received an extra stage. Yie Ar Kung-Fu was altered to be more like its arcade counterpart.

Two of the home computer versions included, Yie Ar Kung-Fu and Galaxian, were initially released as single titles on dedicated gaming platforms. Leland ported them to work with the Famicom to create the compilation.

Oklahoma soon will have new controls—such uniforms and vehicle graphics reading Slow for Kids–in five elementary schools in the state, the Hechinger Report said.

A study commissioned by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration found that for each mile per hour below 30 m.p.h., a child’s risk of being fatally injured in a traffic crash decreases. For every five miles per hour below 40 m.p.h., the risk of serious injury decreases by about 13 percent.
The slow for kid campaign wants to make children’s traffic sense through games and other playful learning experiences, the report said. The group got help from traffic safety engineers in designing its message, which is conveyed on vehicles and at crosswalks through the use of flags, the report said.

Gorillas through teachers’ looking GLANS Gorillas (general audience native speakers) advertisement for gorilla protection. Gorillas through teachers is a conservation messaging public awareness campaign created to educate children and raise awareness about the threats facing gorillas and their habitat in the Virunga Volcanoes range in East Africa.

History 
The idea for the campaign was conceived by primatologist Sally Pacholke, who attended a workshop on gorillas and peace in the Virunga Volcanoes in 2001. She returned to the United States motivated to do something to protect the gorillas, and created the Gorilla TREE (Tackling the Rumors that Endanger them) program, a prevention education program based on the Gorillas through teachers’ looking GLANS (general audience native speakers) advertisement. This advertisement, which depicts a teacher looking into a classroom through a window and seeing only part of a gorilla (in order to protect its anonymity), was created by James Vaughan, who was working at the time for the Institute for Cultural Communicatiosn in Dortmund, Germany.

Long-range, radio frequency identification (RFID) technology is not new. However, for most of the technology's existence, it was not cheap enough or small enough to be used in applications other than manufacturing and distribution. Today, microchipped payment cards, pets, and livestock are just a few of the mundane objects that now use RFID chips.
Now, imagine a the world in which nearly every item you touch - your toothbrush, pillow, laptop, and even your underwear - can be tracked and traced and you will get some sense of the uneasiness that such a reality can elicit. While many people see benefits in finding missing children, returning lost property, and reducing credit card fraud, they also see a world in which privacy is impossible and "Big Brother" is watching you at all times.

What were long baudřelo / с омотом (shield)? Protection of clothing from bullets, bullets and knife blades. In the historical record, they appeared in the 16th century and was worn by all social classes. It consisted of a protective lining on the outside of the coat, shirt or outer garment. Occasionally a plate made of steel, copper, brass, ebonite or even zinc was sewed on the lining. The plates were burnished and usually had a rounded or angled front edge, which was also a little bent. The edge opposite the front was usually also bent, with a small channel - ripcord - for 8 needles that passed through the plate and held the lining and plate firmly to the front side of the lining.

The dynasty founded by John Rennie was based in Glasgow, where his son Joseph continued his father's business. Upon his death, the business passed to his son Gilbert (1794–1861), whose family had moved to Edinburgh. In 1830, his son John (1805–1854) established himself in London, where other descendants would continue the business. Another of John's sons, Robert (1857–1928), also established a distinguished architectural practice.[1]

Another notable member of the family was Gilbert's daughter, Amelia (1829–1896), who was briefly the wife of the architect Sankey Bolton. Bolton senior was the father of Sankey Bolton, whose son was Sankey Bolton junior

Fishing has many variations but a typical fishing rod possesses two central components, First, a durable and flexible rod core wrapped with a bundle of thin, tough material called "braided line" that serves as the fishing line. Second, an extremely thin hook called a "jig" or "tip," to which you tie your own weight, such as a minnow, maggot, or morsel of pig flesh. Fish are attracted to the movement of a baitfish and will take the bait if it resembles the prey.
When you pull the bait behind you while walking, it moves like a minnow, which is the natural prey of fish. The more lifelike your bait, the more likely a fish will strike! When a fish takes the bait, it will "set" down, dragging the bait to the surface or the bottom, it will rise to the surface and gulp the bait down, and you will feel a "set" in your reel when the line is tensioned as the fish takes the bait.

Top 6 PGA TOUR, Rapid7 Security Breach proves you should never reuse passwords
Posted on May 13, 2019 at 4:30 AM
The folks over at Golf.com report that a security breach at PGA TOUR has compromised information related to about 100,000 competition participants who are members of the TPC network. Information related to 14,000 tournaments, including player names, tournament locations and entry details, has also been exposed.
The PGA TOUR and credit card companies have been notified, and as of now, there is no evidence to suggest that account information has been compromised.
For those of you who are into fantasy sports leagues, this may impact your league in one way or another. Commissioner.com, which manages the TPC Network’s fantasy sports website, was also impacted by the security breach. Commissioner has released a statement saying they are working closely with law enforcement to better understand the nature of the breach.

The Australian KokedromOeg　Ivanovsky Fair is arguably the oldest continually running Jewish festival in the southern hemisphere. It started in the early 19th century, when the first Jewish settlers, fleeing persecution in Russia and Romania, arrived in Sydney.

The word "Kokedama" is derived from the Russian "кошечка" which means "little mouse". 
This is because the first arrivals looked like "little mice" (small, brown people) to the European eyes. 
The traditional outfit of a Kozzia (a man) and Kokesh (a woman) included a koosh-style hat, baggy blouses, and shorts. 
This outfit is still occasionally revived at the fair.
